The verse's structural irony: the hypocrites *speak truth* ("you are the messenger of God"), but God *certifies them as liars* — because *they do not believe what they say*. Hypocrisy is not the falsehood of the statement; it is *the falsehood between the statement and the speaker's interior*.

**The strategic disguise.**

[63:2]: "Under the guise of their apparent faith, they repel the people from the path of GOD. Miserable indeed is what they do." — *ittakhadhū aymānahum junnah*. *Junnah* — "shield, cover" — they use *their oaths* as a shield. The cover hides *active obstruction* (*ṣadd `an sabīli llāh*).

**The mechanism.**

[63:3]: "This is because they believed, then disbelieved. Hence, their minds are blocked; they do not understand." — *dhālika bi-annahum āmanū thumma kafarū fa-ṭubi`a `alā qulūbihim fa-hum lā yafqahūn*. *Ṭuba`a* — "stamped, sealed." The hypocrite's *interior comprehension* is *sealed*. They have *belief then disbelief*, and the *cognitive seal* follows.

**The disorienting impression.**

[63:4]: "When you see them, you may be impressed by their looks. And when they speak, you may listen to their eloquence. They are like standing logs. They think that every call is intended against them. These are the real enemies; beware of them. GOD condemns them; they have deviated." — *ka-annahum khushubun musannadah*. *Like propped-up logs* — *outwardly impressive but inwardly hollow / not living*. The hypocrites' *paranoia* — *every call sounds like it's aimed at them* — is the *self-revealing tell* of guilt.

**The hardened refusal.**

[63:5]: "When they are told, 'Come let the messenger of GOD pray for your forgiveness,' they mockingly turn their heads, and you see them repel others and act arrogantly." — *lawwaw ru'ūsahum*. *They twist their heads* — the body-gesture of *contemptuous refusal*. The hypocrite *will not even accept intercession* offered by the Prophet.

[63:6]: "It is the same for them, whether you pray for their forgiveness, or not pray for their forgiveness; GOD will not forgive them. For GOD does not guide the wicked people." — the *closure of the channel*. Once hypocrisy crystallizes to this point, even the Prophet's *istighfār* (forgiveness-prayer) is *equivalent in effect to not praying*.

**The economic-warfare clause.**

[63:7]: "They are the ones who say, 'Do not give any money to those who followed the messenger of GOD, perhaps they abandon him!' However, GOD possesses the treasures of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not comprehend." — the hypocrites' *strategic financial squeeze*: cut off material support from the Muslims, hoping for defection. The verse identifies this *exact tactic* historically employed by ibn Ubayy during the campaign against Banū al-Muṣṭaliq.

**The threat — and its inversion.**

[63:8]: "They say, 'If we go back to the city, the powerful therein will evict the weak (and we will be victimized)!' (They should know that) all dignity belongs to GOD and His messenger, and the believers. However, the hypocrites do not know." — the verse preserves the historical threat almost verbatim (ibn Ubayy's "the powerful shall expel the weak from Medina"). The Quranic *inversion*: *al-`izzah li-llāh wa-li-rasūlihī wa-li-l-mu'minīn* — "all dignity belongs to GOD and His messenger and the believers." The hypocrite's claim to dignity is *reversed* in three names.

**The instruction to the believers.**

[63:9]: "O you who believe, do not be distracted by your money and your children from remembering GOD. Those who do this are the losers." — *lā tulhikum amwālukum wa-lā awlādukum `an dhikri llāh*. *Wealth + children* as the structural *distractions* — the same pair flagged again in [64:14]–[64:15] as *fitnah* (test).

**The end-of-life regret.**

[63:10]: "You shall give from our provisions to you before death comes to you, then you say, 'My Lord, if only You could delay this for a short while! I would then be charitable and join the righteous!'" — the *pre-emptive give* before the dying-bed regret. The hypothetical *posthumous request* — *delay me a little longer* — is uniformly *denied*:

[63:11]: "GOD never delays the appointed time of death for any soul. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do." — *wa-lan yu'akhkhira llāhu nafsan idhā jā'a ajaluhā*. The *ajal* (appointed time) is *unmovable*. The closing line of the surah converts the hypocrite-warning into a *universal* memento mori: do your charity *now*.

**Synthesis.**

Hypocrisy is the *truth-statement uttered by a non-believer* ([63:1]); it operates by *oath-as-shield* ([63:2]); the cognitive seal follows the *believed-then-disbelieved* pattern ([63:3]); the hypocrite is *outwardly impressive*, inwardly hollow ([63:4]); even the Prophet's forgiveness-prayer cannot reach them once the channel is closed ([63:5]–[63:6]); their *strategic moves* include *financial pressure* on believers ([63:7]); their *threats of expelling the weak* are reversed by God's verdict ([63:8]); the believers are warned against *wealth and children as distraction* ([63:9]); and the surah closes with *the universal memento mori* — death is on schedule; *give now* ([63:10]–[63:11]).

63:1.text: When the hypocrites come to you they say, "We bear witness that you are the messenger of GOD." GOD knows that you are His messenger, and GOD bears witness that the hypocrites are liars.
63:1.arabic: إذا جاءك المنفقون قالوا نشهد إنك لرسول الله والله يعلم إنك لرسوله والله يشهد إن المنفقين لكذبون

63:2.text: Under the guise of their apparent faith, they repel the people from the path of GOD. Miserable indeed is what they do.
63:2.arabic: اتخذوا أيمنهم جنة فصدوا عن سبيل الله إنهم ساء ما كانوا يعملون

63:3.text: This is because they believed, then disbelieved. Hence, their minds are blocked; they do not understand.
63:3.arabic: ذلك بأنهم ءامنوا ثم كفروا فطبع على قلوبهم فهم لا يفقهون

63:4.text: When you see them, you may be impressed by their looks. And when they speak, you may listen to their eloquence. They are like standing logs. They think that every call is intended against them. These are the real enemies; beware of them. GOD condemns them; they have deviated.
63:4.arabic: ۞وإذا رأيتهم تعجبك أجسامهم وإن يقولوا تسمع لقولهم كأنهم خشب مسندة يحسبون كل صيحة عليهم هم العدو فاحذرهم قتلهم الله أنى يؤفكون

63:5.text: When they are told, "Come let the messenger of GOD pray for your forgiveness," they mockingly turn their heads, and you see them repel others and act arrogantly.
63:5.arabic: وإذا قيل لهم تعالوا يستغفر لكم رسول الله لووا رءوسهم ورأيتهم يصدون وهم مستكبرون

63:6.text: It is the same for them, whether you pray for their forgiveness, or not pray for their forgiveness; GOD will not forgive them. For GOD does not guide the wicked people.
63:6.arabic: سواء عليهم أستغفرت لهم أم لم تستغفر لهم لن يغفر الله لهم إن الله لا يهدي القوم الفسقين

63:7.text: They are the ones who say, "Do not give any money to those who followed the messenger of GOD, perhaps they abandon him!" However, GOD possesses the treasures of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites do not comprehend.
63:7.arabic: هم الذين يقولون لا تنفقوا على من عند رسول الله حتى ينفضوا ولله خزائن السموت والأرض ولكن المنفقين لا يفقهون

63:8.text: They say, "If we go back to the city, the powerful therein will evict the weak (and we will be victimized)." (They should know that) all dignity belongs to GOD and His messenger, and the believers. However, the hypocrites do not know.
63:8.arabic: يقولون لئن رجعنا إلى المدينة ليخرجن الأعز منها الأذل ولله العزة ولرسوله وللمؤمنين ولكن المنفقين لا يعلمون

63:9.text: O you who believe, do not be distracted by your money and your children from remembering GOD. Those who do this are the losers.
63:9.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لا تلهكم أمولكم ولا أولدكم عن ذكر الله ومن يفعل ذلك فأولـئك هم الخسرون

63:10.text: You shall give from our provisions to you before death comes to you, then you say, "My Lord, if only You could delay this for a short while! I would then be charitable and join the righteous!"
63:10.arabic: وأنفقوا من ما رزقنكم من قبل أن يأتي أحدكم الموت فيقول رب لولا أخرتني إلى أجل قريب فأصدق وأكن من الصـلحين

63:11.text: GOD never delays the appointed time of death for any soul. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
63:11.arabic: ولن يؤخر الله نفسا إذا جاء أجلها والله خبير بما تعملون

question: Explain Surah 98 (Proof) verse by verse.
answer:
Surah 98 (Al-Bayyinah — "Proof") is eight verses, Medinan in the Khalifa identification (some classical lists put it as Meccan; rhythm and content read Medinan). Short, dense, and one of the Quran's most direct statements of *what religion actually requires*.

**The opening — the disbelievers and their *waiting-for-proof* posture.**

[98:1]: "Those who disbelieved among the people of the scripture, as well as the idol worshipers, insist on their ways, despite the proof given to them." — *lam yakuni lladhīna kafarū min ahli l-kitābi wa-l-mushrikīna munfakkīna ḥattā ta'tiyahumu l-bayyinah*. Khalifa's rendering preserves the syntactic structure: the *people of scripture-disbelievers* + *idol-worshipers* are *not unbinding* (their conviction is *firm in error*) until *the proof* arrives. The implicit complaint: they were *waiting* for a proof, then refused to recognize it when it came.

[98:2]: "A messenger from GOD is reciting to them sacred instructions." — *rasūlun mina llāhi yatlū ṣuḥufan muṭahharah*. *Muṭahharah* — *purified*. The instructions are *purified scrolls*.

[98:3]: "In them there are valuable teachings." — *fīhā kutubun qayyimah*. *Qayyim* — *upright, stable, lasting*.

**The historical diagnosis.**

[98:4]: "In fact, those who received the scripture did not dispute except after the proof was given to them." — *wa-mā tafarraqa lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba illā min ba`di mā jā'athumu l-bayyinah*. The historical claim: previous scripture-receivers *did not split into factions* until *after* clear evidence arrived. Division is *not a function of insufficient evidence* but of *response to evidence*.

**The operational definition of religion.**

[98:5]: "All that was asked of them was to worship GOD, devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone, observe the contact prayers (Salat), and give the obligatory charity (Zakat). Such is the perfect religion." — *wa-mā umirū illā li-ya`budū llāha mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīn ḥunafā', wa-yuqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-yu'tū z-zakāt, wa-dhālika dīnu l-qayyimah*.

This verse is one of the Quran's most-cited statements on *the irreducible religious core*: (1) worship God *alone*, (2) devoting religion *purely* to Him (*mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīn*), (3) as *upright monotheists* (*ḥunafā'*), (4) establishing *Ṣalāt*, (5) giving *Zakāt*. Five components — and *that is the religion of uprightness*. Khalifa frequently invokes this verse against ritual additions and post-Quranic doctrinal accretions: the *operational religion* is named explicitly, and nothing more is required.

**The forks.**

[98:6]: "Those who disbelieved among the people of the scripture, and the idol worshipers, have incurred the fire of Gehenna forever. They are the worst creatures." — *ulā'ika hum sharru l-bariyyah*. *The disbelievers among scripture-people and idol-worshipers* — *worst of creatures*.

[98:7]: "Those who believed and led a righteous life are the best creatures." — *ulā'ika hum khayru l-bariyyah*. *The believers and the righteous* — *best of creatures*. The verse-pair is one of the Quran's most direct *moral ranking* statements.

**The reward.**

[98:8]: "Their reward at their Lord is the gardens of Eden with flowing streams, wherein they abide forever. GOD is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. Such is the reward for those who reverence their Lord." — *raḍiya llāhu `anhum wa-raḍū `anhu*. *God is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him* — the *mutual contentment* formula, also in [89:28] and [5:119]. The closing clause: *dhālika li-man khashiya rabbahū* — *that is for one who reverences his Lord*. The reward is conditional on *khashyah* — reverent awe.

**Synthesis.**

In eight verses the surah states: the proof has come; division among scripture-receivers is *post-proof*, not from insufficient evidence ([98:4]); the *irreducible religion* is monotheistic devotion + Ṣalāt + Zakāt ([98:5]); disbelievers of scripture-people and idolaters are *worst of creatures*, believers are *best of creatures* ([98:6]–[98:7]); the eternal reward is gardens with *mutual contentment* — *God pleased with them and they pleased with Him* — *for those who reverence their Lord* ([98:8]). The surah is a one-page-long irreducible-religion catechism.

98:1.text: Those who disbelieved among the people of the scripture, as well as the idol worshipers, insist on their ways, despite the proof given to them.
98:1.arabic: لم يكن الذين كفروا من أهل الكتب والمشركين منفكين حتى تأتيهم البينة

98:2.text: A messenger from GOD is reciting to them sacred instructions.
98:2.arabic: رسول من الله يتلوا صحفا مطهرة

98:3.text: In them there are valuable teachings.

98:4.text: In fact, those who received the scripture did not dispute until the proof was given to them.
98:4.arabic: وما تفرق الذين أوتوا الكتب إلا من بعد ما جاءتهم البينة

98:5.text: All that was asked of them was to worship GOD, devoting the religion absolutely to Him alone, observe the contact prayers (Salat), and give the obligatory charity (Zakat). Such is the perfect religion.
98:5.arabic: وما أمروا إلا ليعبدوا الله مخلصين له الدين حنفاء ويقيموا الصلوة ويؤتوا الزكوة وذلك دين القيمة

98:6.text: Those who disbelieved among the people of the scripture, and the idol worshipers, have incurred the fire of Gehenna forever. They are the worst creatures.
98:6.arabic: إن الذين كفروا من أهل الكتب والمشركين في نار جهنم خلدين فيها أولـئك هم شر البرية

98:7.text: Those who believed and led a righteous life are the best creatures.
98:7.arabic: إن الذين ءامنوا وعملوا الصـلحت أولـئك هم خير البرية

98:8.text: Their reward at their Lord is the gardens of Eden with flowing streams, wherein they abide forever. GOD is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. Such is the reward for those who reverence their Lord.
98:8.arabic: جزاؤهم عند ربهم جنـت عدن تجري من تحتها الأنهر خلدين فيها أبدا رضي الله عنهم ورضوا عنه ذلك لمن خشي ربه

question: Explain Surah 82 (The Shattering) verse by verse.
answer:
Surah 82 (Al-Infiṭār — "The Shattering") is nineteen verses, Meccan. The surah is a tight companion-piece to surah 81 (At-Takwīr) — both open with a serial *idhā* ("when") + verb sequence describing cosmic dissolution, both have a midpoint that reveals what each soul will know, and both close with a memorial-style reminder. Surah 82's distinctive contribution is its central verse-pair on the *honorable recording angels*.

**The dissolution sequence (82:1–4).**

[82:1]: "When the heaven is shattered." — *idhā s-samā'u nfaṭarat*. *Infaṭara* = "to be split / shattered" — *fa-ṭ-r* root (the same as *fiṭrah*, the *original disposition*). The verb is sometimes glossed as the heaven's *cracking open* at the cosmic event.

[82:2]: "The planets are scattered." — *wa-idhā l-kawākibu ntatharat*. *Intathara* — "to be scattered, broken up." The planetary order *unravels*.

[82:3]: "The oceans explode." — *wa-idhā l-biḥāru fujjirat*. *Fujjira* — *cleft, made to burst forth*. Companion to [81:6]'s *the oceans set aflame*; both image the seas as *opened* / *erupting*.

[82:4]: "The graves are opened." — *wa-idhā l-qubūru bu`thirat*. *Bu`thira* — *overturned, scattered*. The graves' contents *come out*.

**The soul's accounting.**

[82:5]: "Every soul will find out what caused it to advance, and what caused it to regress." — *`alimat nafsun mā qaddamat wa-akhkharat*. *Each soul knows what it sent ahead and what it deferred*. The two verbs — *qaddama* (sent forward, ahead-of-itself) and *akhkhara* (deferred, held back) — are *the moral economy in a single line*.

**The address to the unappreciative human.**

[82:6]: "O you human being, what diverted you from your Lord Most Honorable?" — *yā ayyuhā l-insānu mā gharraka bi-rabbika l-karīm*. *Mā gharraka* — *what deceived you / what fooled you*? The accusation is gentle and pointed: the *karīm* (most generous) is the One you were diverted *from*.

[82:7]: "The One who created you, designed you, and perfected you." — *alladhī khalaqaka fa-sawwāka fa-`adalaka*. Three verbs of formation: *created, proportioned, made-balanced*.

[82:8]: "In whatever design He chose, He constructed you." — *fī ayyi ṣūratin mā shā'a rakkabaka*. The *form* of each individual is by *divine choice*.

**The honorable recording angels (82:9–12).**

[82:9]: "Indeed, you disbelieve in the religion." — *kallā, bal tukadhdhibūna bi-d-dīn*. *Dīn* here = "religion / judgment / accounting." The diagnosis: *you deny the accounting*.

[82:10]: "Oblivious to the fact that there are (invisible) keepers around you." — *wa-inna `alaykum la-ḥāfiẓīn*. *Keepers* over you.

[82:11]: "They are honest recorders." — *kirāman kātibīn*. *Noble scribes*. This is the foundational verse for the *kirāman kātibīn* in Quranic angelology — the noble recording angels assigned to each soul (cross-reference [50:17]–[50:18]).

[82:12]: "They know everything you do." — *ya`lamūna mā taf`alūn*. The recorders' knowledge is *complete*.

**The two destinations.**

[82:13]: "Surely, the pious will end up in bliss." — *inna l-abrāra la-fī na`īm*. *Abrār* — *the truly upright*.

[82:14]: "While the wicked will be in Hell." — *wa-inna l-fujjāra la-fī jaḥīm*. *Fujjār* — *the dissolute*.

**The Day named.**

[82:15]: "Will incur it on the Day of Judgment." — *yaṣlawnahā yawma d-dīn*. They *roast in it* on the day of accounting.

[82:16]: "They never leave it." — *wa-mā hum `anhā bi-ghā'ibīn*. *No absenting* — they are not absent from it.

**The rhetorical climax.**

[82:17]: "Awesome is the Day of Judgment." — *wa-mā adrāka mā yawmu d-dīn*. *Do you know what the day of accounting is*?

[82:18]: "Awesome indeed is the Day of Judgment." — repeated. The double rhetorical question is a Quranic intensification-device.

[82:19]: "That is the day when no soul can help another soul, and all decisions, on that day, will belong to GOD." — *yawma lā tamliku nafsun li-nafsin shay'an wa-l-amru yawma'idhin li-llāh*. The closing verdict: *no soul controls anything for another soul; the command on that day is God's*.

**Synthesis.**

The heavens shatter, planets scatter, oceans burst, graves open ([82:1]–[82:4]); each soul knows what it *sent ahead* and what it *deferred* ([82:5]); the address turns to *you, human* — *what deceived you about the Most Generous?* ([82:6]); the answer involves *forgetting* the *noble recorders* ([82:10]–[82:12]); the pious end in bliss, the dissolute in Gehenna ([82:13]–[82:14]); and the closing verse withdraws *all soul-to-soul help* — *the command on that day is God's* ([82:19]).

82:5.text: Every soul will find out what caused it to advance, and what caused it to regress. Reflect on God's Creations

82:6.text: O you human being, what diverted you from your Lord Most Honorable?
82:6.arabic: يـأيها الإنسن ما غرك بربك الكريم

82:7.text: The One who created you, designed you, and perfected you.

82:8.text: In whatever design He chose, He constructed it.
82:8.arabic: في أي صورة ما شاء ركبك

82:9.text: Indeed, you disbelieve in the religion.

82:10.text: Oblivious to the fact that there are (invisible) keepers around you.

82:13.text: Surely, the pious have deserved bliss.

82:14.text: While the wicked have deserved Hell.

82:15.text: Will incur it on the Day of Judgment.

82:17.text: Awesome is the Day of Judgment.

82:18.text: What a day; the Day of Judgment!
82:18.arabic: ثم ما أدرىك ما يوم الدين

82:19.text: That is the day when no soul can help another soul, and all decisions, on that day, will belong to GOD.
82:19.arabic: يوم لا تملك نفس لنفس شيـا والأمر يومئذ لله

question: Explain Surah 61 (The Column) verse by verse.
answer:
Surah 61 (Aṣ-Ṣaff — "The Column" / "The Row") is fourteen verses, Medinan. The surah's title comes from [61:4], where God names the kind of believer He loves: *those who fight in His cause united in one column, like bricks in a wall*. The surah contains one of the Quran's most contested passages — [61:6], in which Jesus prophesies a future messenger named *Aḥmad* — and a clear command to *match your speech with your deeds*.

**The opening — universal glorification.**

[61:1]: "Glorifying GOD is everything in the heavens and everything on earth. He is the Almighty, Most Wise." — *sabbaḥa li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ*. The opening claim — *creation glorifies God* — recurs across the *Musabbiḥāt* (the seven *glorification-opening* surahs: 57, 59, 61, 62, 64, plus the perfect tense in 87:1 and 17:44).

**The word-deed alignment requirement.**

[61:2]: "O you who believe, why do you say what you do not do?" — *li-ma taqūlūna mā lā taf`alūn*. The direct second-person rebuke addressed to *the believers* — not the disbelievers, not the hypocrites — *the believers themselves* are warned against speech-act mismatch.

[61:3]: "Most abominable in the sight of GOD is that you say what you do not do." — *kabura maqtan `inda llāh*. *Maqt* — *intense displeasure, hatred*. *Speech-deed mismatch is the thing God most-hates in His own believers.*

**The wall-of-soldiers image.**

[61:4]: "GOD loves those who fight in His cause united in one column, like the bricks in one wall." — *yuḥibbu lladhīna yuqātilūna fī sabīlihī ṣaffan ka-annahum bunyānun marṣūṣ*. *Marṣūṣ* — *welded, fused, mortared together*. The believer-army is imaged as *a single welded wall* — not a *crowd* but a *structural unit*.

**The Mosaic precedent.**

[61:5]: "Recall that Moses said to his people, 'O my people, why do you hurt me, even though you know that I am GOD's messenger to you?' When they deviated, GOD diverted their hearts. For GOD does not guide the wicked people." — Moses's *own people* hurt him *despite* his certified messenger-status. The historical analogue serves to *normalize* the Prophet's experience of resistance from his own community.

**The Christological prophecy.**

[61:6]: "Recall that Jesus, son of Mary, said, 'O Children of Israel, I am GOD's messenger to you, confirming the Torah and bringing good news of a messenger to come after me whose name will be even more praised (Ahmad).' Then, when he showed them the clear proofs, they said, 'This is profound magic.'" — *wa-mubashshiran bi-rasūlin ya'tī min ba`dī ismuhū Aḥmad*. The verse identifies *Aḥmad* — a name from the same root as *Muḥammad* (Ḥ-M-D, "to praise") — as the *post-Jesus* messenger. *Aḥmad* is the comparative-form: *more praised*. Khalifa preserves the standard Muslim reading that this is a prediction of Muhammad's coming; the verse is also one of the Quran's two principal *christological* claims (alongside surahs 3, 4, 5, 19) — Jesus is *messenger to the Israelites*, confirmer of Torah, bringer of news of a successor.

**The light cannot be put out.**

[61:7]: "Who is more evil than one who fabricates lies about GOD, and he is being invited to Submission? GOD does not guide the evil people." — *iftirā'* (fabrication-of-lies-about-God) is the worst category.

[61:8]: "They wish to put out GOD's light with their mouths. But GOD insists upon perfecting His light, in spite of the disbelievers." — *yurīdūna li-yuṭfi'ū nūra llāhi bi-afwāhihim*. The image: *blowing on God's light to extinguish it*. The image *guarantees* the absurdity.

[61:9]: "He has sent His messenger with the guidance and the true religion, and will make it dominate all religions, in spite of the idol worshipers." — the verse appears verbatim in [9:33] and [48:28] — a *triple* repetition unique in the Quran. The promise: *the true religion will prevail over all religions*.

**The trade-that-saves.**

[61:10]: "O you who believe, let Me inform you of a trade that will save you from painful retribution." — *hal adullukum `alā tijāratin tunjīkum*. The *commerce* metaphor — *I will guide you to a trade that saves from painful retribution*.

[61:11]: "Believe in GOD and His messenger and strive in the cause of GOD with your money and your lives. This is the best deal for you, if you only knew." — *tu'minūna bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihī wa-tujāhidūna fī sabīli llāh*. The *purchase*: belief + striving with money and lives.

[61:12]: "In return, He forgives your sins, and admits you into gardens with flowing streams, with beautiful mansions in the gardens of Eden. This is the greatest triumph." — the *return*: forgiveness, paradise, *masākin ṭayyiba* (good dwellings) in Eden.

[61:13]: "Additionally, you get something you truly love: support from GOD and guaranteed victory. Give good news to the believers." — the *bonus*: this-life *support and imminent victory* (*naṣrun mina llāhi wa-fatḥun qarīb*).

**The disciples-of-Jesus call.**

[61:14]: "O you who believe, be GOD's supporters, like the disciples of Jesus, son of Mary. When he said to them, 'Who are my supporters towards GOD,' they said, 'We are GOD's supporters.' Thus, a group from the Children of Israel believed, and another group disbelieved. We helped those who believed against their enemy, until they won." — the *ḥawāriyyūn* of Jesus are presented as *the model*. *Anṣār Allāh* — *helpers of God* — the same term that Medinans took for themselves vis-à-vis the Prophet (*Anṣār*). The surah closes by drawing a *direct line* from the disciples-of-Jesus to the believing community now being addressed.

**Synthesis.**

All creation glorifies God ([61:1]); believers must *match speech with deed* ([61:2]–[61:3]); when they do fight, they fight *as a single welded wall* ([61:4]); Moses and Jesus both faced internal opposition ([61:5]–[61:6]) — and Jesus *predicted Aḥmad*; God's light cannot be blown out ([61:8]); the trade that saves is *belief + striving with money and lives* ([61:10]–[61:13]); and the model for the helpers-of-God is *the disciples of Jesus* ([61:14]).

61:1.text: Glorifying GOD is everything in the heavens and everything on earth. He is the Almighty, Most Wise.
61:1.arabic: سبح لله ما في السموت وما في الأرض وهو العزيز الحكيم

61:2.text: O you who believe, why do you say what you do not do?
61:2.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا لم تقولون ما لا تفعلون

61:3.text: Most abominable in the sight of GOD is that you say what you do not do.
61:3.arabic: كبر مقتا عند الله أن تقولوا ما لا تفعلون

61:4.text: GOD loves those who fight in His cause united in one column, like the bricks in one wall.
61:4.arabic: إن الله يحب الذين يقتلون في سبيله صفا كأنهم بنين مرصوص

61:5.text: Recall that Moses said to his people, "O my people, why do you hurt me, even though you know that I am GOD's messenger to you?" When they deviated, GOD diverted their hearts. For GOD does not guide the wicked people.
61:5.arabic: وإذ قال موسى لقومه يقوم لم تؤذونني وقد تعلمون أني رسول الله إليكم فلما زاغوا أزاغ الله قلوبهم والله لا يهدي القوم الفسقين

61:6.text: Recall that Jesus, son of Mary, said, "O Children of Israel, I am GOD's messenger to you, confirming the Torah and bringing good news of a messenger to come after me whose name will be even more praised (Ahmad)." Then, when he showed them the clear proofs, they said, "This is profound magic."
61:6.arabic: وإذ قال عيسى ابن مريم يبني إسرـءيل إني رسول الله إليكم مصدقا لما بين يدي من التورىة ومبشرا برسول يأتي من بعدي اسمه أحمد فلما جاءهم بالبينت قالوا هذا سحر مبين

61:7.text: Who is more evil than one who fabricates lies about GOD, and he is being invited to Submission? GOD does not guide the evil people.
61:7.arabic: ومن أظلم ممن افترى على الله الكذب وهو يدعى إلى الإسلم والله لا يهدي القوم الظـلمين

61:8.text: They wish to put out GOD's light with their mouths. But GOD insists upon perfecting His light, in spite of the disbelievers.
61:8.arabic: يريدون ليطفـوا نور الله بأفوههم والله متم نوره ولو كره الكفرون

61:9.text: He has sent His messenger with the guidance and the true reliin spite of the idol gion, and will make it dominate all religions, worshipers.
61:9.arabic: هو الذي أرسل رسوله بالهدى ودين الحق ليظهره على الدين كله ولو كره المشركون

61:10.text: O you who believe, let Me inform you of a trade that will save you from painful retribution.
61:10.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا هل أدلكم على تجرة تنجيكم من عذاب أليم

61:11.text: Believe in GOD and His messenger and strive in the cause of GOD with your money and your lives. This is the best deal for you, if you only knew.
61:11.arabic: تؤمنون بالله ورسوله وتجهدون في سبيل الله بأمولكم وأنفسكم ذلكم خير لكم إن كنتم تعلمون

61:12.text: In return, He forgives your sins, and admits you into gardens with flowing streams, with beautiful mansions in the gardens of Eden. This is the greatest triumph.
61:12.arabic: يغفر لكم ذنوبكم ويدخلكم جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر ومسكن طيبة في جنـت عدن ذلك الفوز العظيم

61:13.text: Additionally, you get something you truly love: support from GOD and guaranteed victory. Give good news to the believers.
61:13.arabic: وأخرى تحبونها نصر من الله وفتح قريب وبشر المؤمنين

61:14.text: O you who believe, be GOD's supporters, like the disciples of Jesus, son of Mary. When he said to them, "Who are my supporters towards GOD," they said, "We are GOD's supporters." Thus, a group from the Children of Israel believed, and another group disbelieved. We helped those who believed against their enemy, until they won.
61:14.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا كونوا أنصار الله كما قال عيسى ابن مريم للحواريـن من أنصاري إلى الله قال الحواريون نحن أنصار الله فـامنت طائفة من بني إسرـءيل وكفرت طائفة فأيدنا الذين ءامنوا على عدوهم فأصبحوا ظهرين

question: What is the central message of Surah 58 (The Debate)?
answer:
Surah 58 (Al-Mujādilah — "The Debate" / "The Disputant Woman") is twenty-two verses, Medinan. The surah opens with a specific woman's complaint against her husband over an Arabian practice called *ẓihār* (a pre-Islamic divorce-formula); the Quran responds first to *that specific complaint*, then expands to a broader theme: *God hears, God is present, God's party wins*.

**The opening — the woman who debated.**

[58:1]: "GOD has heard the woman who debated with you about her husband, and complained to GOD. GOD heard everything the two of you discussed. GOD is Hearer, Seer." — *qad sami`a llāhu qawla llatī tujādiluka fī zawjihā*. Khawla bint Tha`labah (classical identification) had been *zihāred* by her husband — pronounced by him as *like the back of my mother* — a pre-Islamic divorce-formula leaving the wife *neither married nor divorced*, frozen. She came to the Prophet, who initially could not give her relief; she appealed *to God*. The opening verse is God *answering her* by *naming the hearing*. The surah's title — *al-mujādilah* (the woman who debated) — comes from her.

**The reform of *ẓihār*.**

[58:2]–[58:4]: "Those among you who estrange their wives (by declaring them as forbidden in sex) as their mothers know full well that they are not their mothers. Their mothers are the women who gave birth to them. Indeed, they are committing a blasphemy and a falsehood. GOD is Pardoner, Forgiver. Those who estrange their wives in this manner, then reconcile thereafter, shall atone by freeing a slave before resuming their sexual relations. This is to enlighten you. GOD is Cognizant of everything you do. If you cannot find a slave to free, you shall fast two consecutive months before resuming sexual relations. If you cannot fast, then you shall feed sixty poor people. You shall believe in GOD and His messenger. These are GOD's laws. The disbelievers have incurred a painful retribution." — the Quran *abolishes* the *ẓihār* status as a divorce-mechanism and provides a *graded expiation* (free a slave → two months' fasting → feed sixty poor) for reconciliation. The reform is one of the Quran's most concrete legal interventions in pre-Islamic Arabian custom — and its origin is *one woman's complaint*.

**The general principle — God hears.**

[58:5]: "Surely, those who fight GOD and His messenger are committed to defeat, like their previous counterparts were committed to defeat. We have sent down clear proofs, and the rejectors have incurred a shameful retribution." — *yuḥāddūna llāha wa-rasūlahū* — *those who oppose God and His messenger*. The verb *ḥādda* contains the same root as *ḥadd* (limit / boundary), suggesting *those who set themselves against the boundary*.

**The omnipresence theology.**

[58:7]: "Do you not realize that GOD knows everything in the heavens and everything on earth? No three people can conspire secretly without Him being their fourth, nor five without Him being the sixth, nor less than that, nor more, without Him being there with them wherever they may be. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, He will inform them of everything they had done. GOD is fully aware of all things." — *mā yakūnu min najwā thalāthatin illā huwa rābi`uhum*. The *numerical* construction: *no three conspire without Him being their fourth*, *no five without Him being their sixth*. The verse is one of the Quran's strongest statements of *God's presence with the conspirators*. This is the surah's *central theological hinge*: God hears the woman's complaint, and God is also present at every secret conspiracy.

**The secret-conspiracy theme.**

[58:8]–[58:10]: Those who whisper *to commit sin and disobey the messenger* greet the Prophet with deformed greetings ("Sām `alaykum" instead of "Salām `alaykum") and trust themselves that *God will not punish them for their utterances*. The believers are warned: *if you confer secretly, confer to work righteousness and piety*. *Secret conspiracy is the devil's idea*.

**The make-room courtesy and the rank-of-knowledge.**

[58:11]: "O you who believe, if you are told, 'Please make room,' you shall make room for each other to sit. GOD will then make room for you. If you are asked to get up and move, get up and move. GOD raises those among you who believe, and those who acquire knowledge to higher ranks. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do." — the *make-room* etiquette in the Prophet's gathering, *then* the larger ethic: *make room for each other and God will make room for you*. The closing clause raises *believers and knowledge-acquirers* to higher ranks.

**The conferring-with-the-messenger charity.**

[58:12]–[58:13]: A *graduated* practice — those who wish to *confer privately* with the Prophet should *first give to charity*. The practice is later relaxed; the verse remains as evidence of the *priority-setting* technique.

**The friendship test.**

[58:14]: "Have you noted those who befriended people with whom GOD is angry? They neither belong with you, nor with them. They deliberately swear lies!" — the *liminal* class: friending *those God is angry with* makes you *neither with us nor with them*.

**God's party wins.**

[58:19]: "The devil has possessed them, and has caused them to disregard GOD's message. These are the party of the devil. Absolutely, the party of the devil are the losers." — *ḥizbu sh-shayṭān* — *the party of Satan*. The losers.

[58:20]: "Surely, those who oppose GOD and His messenger will be with the lowliest."

[58:21]: "GOD has decreed: 'I and My messengers will most assuredly win.' GOD is Powerful, Almighty." — *kataba llāhu la-aghlibanna anā wa-rusulī*. *God has decreed: I and My messengers shall absolutely conquer.* The verse is one of the Quran's most decisive *outcome-guarantees*.

[58:22]: "You will not find people who believe in GOD and the Last Day befriending those who oppose GOD and His messenger, even if they were their parents, or their children, or their siblings, or their tribe. For these, He decrees faith into their hearts, and supports them with inspiration from Him, and admits them into gardens with flowing streams wherein they abide forever. GOD is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. These are the party of GOD. Most assuredly, GOD's party are the winners." — *ḥizbu llāh* — *God's party*. The closing line of the surah is the *one named opposite* of [58:19]'s *party of Satan*. The closing description: *He decrees faith into their hearts, supports them with inspiration*. The contrast: *Satan possesses* the disbelievers; *God supports* the believers with His own inspiration.

**Central message.**

God hears the woman who appealed to Him about her husband ([58:1]); He reforms the pre-Islamic *ẓihār* divorce-formula via a graded expiation ([58:2]–[58:4]); He is *present with every secret conspiracy as the next number* ([58:7]); secret-speech should be *for righteousness*, not for sin ([58:9]); social etiquette in the Prophet's gathering is *make-room courtesy* and *give before you confer* ([58:11]–[58:12]); friendship with God's enemies puts you *neither here nor there* ([58:14]); the party of Satan loses, the party of God wins, *guaranteed by decree* ([58:19], [58:21]–[58:22]).

58:1.text: GOD has heard the woman who debated with you about her husband, and complained to GOD. GOD heard everything the two of you discussed. GOD is Hearer, Seer.
58:1.arabic: قد سمع الله قول التي تجدلك في زوجها وتشتكي إلى الله والله يسمع تحاوركما إن الله سميع بصير

58:2.text: Those among you who estrange their wives (by declaring them as forbidden in sex) as their mothers know full well that they are not their mothers. Their mothers are the women who gave birth to them. Indeed, they are committing a blasphemy and a falsehood. GOD is Pardoner, Forgiver.
58:2.arabic: الذين يظهرون منكم من نسائهم ما هن أمهتهم إن أمهتهم إلا الــي ولدنهم وإنهم ليقولون منكرا من القول وزورا وإن الله لعفو غفور

58:3.text: Those who estrange their wives in this manner, then reconcile thereafter, shall atone by freeing a slave before resuming their sexual relations. This is to enlighten you. GOD is Cognizant of everything you do.
58:3.arabic: والذين يظهرون من نسائهم ثم يعودون لما قالوا فتحرير رقبة من قبل أن يتماسا ذلكم توعظون به والله بما تعملون خبير

58:4.text: If you cannot find a slave to free, you shall fast two consecutive months before resuming sexual relations. If you cannot fast, then you shall feed sixty poor people. You shall believe in GOD and His messenger. These are GOD's laws. The disbelievers have incurred a painful retribution.
58:4.arabic: فمن لم يجد فصيام شهرين متتابعين من قبل أن يتماسا فمن لم يستطع فإطعام ستين مسكينا ذلك لتؤمنوا بالله ورسوله وتلك حدود الله وللكفرين عذاب أليم

58:5.text: Surely, those who fight GOD and His messenger are committed to defeat, like their previous counterparts were committed to defeat. We have sent down clear proofs, and the rejectors have incurred a shameful retribution.
58:5.arabic: إن الذين يحادون الله ورسوله كبتوا كما كبت الذين من قبلهم وقد أنزلنا ءايت بينت وللكفرين عذاب مهين

58:7.text: Do you not realize that GOD knows everything in the heavens and everything on earth? No three people can conspire secretly without Him being their fourth, nor five without Him being the sixth, nor less than that, nor more, without Him being there with them wherever they may be. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, He will inform them of everything they had done. GOD is fully aware of all things.
58:7.arabic: ألم تر أن الله يعلم ما في السموت وما في الأرض ما يكون من نجوى ثلثة إلا هو رابعهم ولا خمسة إلا هو سادسهم ولا أدنى من ذلك ولا أكثر إلا هو معهم أين ما كانوا ثم ينبئهم بما عملوا يوم القيمة إن الله بكل شيء عليم

58:9.text: O you who believe, if you have to confer secretly, you shall not confer to commit sin, transgression, and to disobey the messenger. You shall confer to work righteousness and piety. You shall reverence GOD, before whom you will be summoned.
58:9.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إذا تنجيتم فلا تتنجوا بالإثم والعدون ومعصيت الرسول وتنجوا بالبر والتقوى واتقوا الله الذي إليه تحشرون

58:11.text: O you who believe, if you are told, "Please make room," you shall make room for each other to sit. GOD will then make room for you. If you are asked to get up and move, get up and move. GOD raises those among you who believe, and those who acquire knowledge to higher ranks. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
58:11.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إذا قيل لكم تفسحوا في المجلس فافسحوا يفسح الله لكم وإذا قيل انشزوا فانشزوا يرفع الله الذين ءامنوا منكم والذين أوتوا العلم درجت والله بما تعملون خبير

58:12.text: O you who believe, when you wish to confer with the messenger, you shall offer a charity (to the poor) before you do so. This is better for you, and purer. If you cannot afford it, then GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
58:12.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إذا نجيتم الرسول فقدموا بين يدي نجوىكم صدقة ذلك خير لكم وأطهر فإن لم تجدوا فإن الله غفور رحيم

58:14.text: Have you noted those who befriended people with whom GOD is angry? They neither belong with you, nor with them. They deliberately swear lies!
58:14.arabic: ۞ألم تر إلى الذين تولوا قوما غضب الله عليهم ما هم منكم ولا منهم ويحلفون على الكذب وهم يعلمون

58:19.text: The devil has possessed them, and has caused them to disregard GOD's message. These are the party of the devil. Absolutely, the party of the devil are the losers.
58:19.arabic: استحوذ عليهم الشيطن فأنسىهم ذكر الله أولـئك حزب الشيطن ألا إن حزب الشيطن هم الخسرون

58:20.text: Surely, those who oppose GOD and His messenger will be with the lowliest.
58:20.arabic: إن الذين يحادون الله ورسوله أولـئك في الأذلين

58:21.text: GOD has decreed: "I and My messengers will most assuredly win." GOD is Powerful, Almighty.
58:21.arabic: كتب الله لأغلبن أنا ورسلي إن الله قوي عزيز

58:22.text: You will not find people who believe in GOD and the Last Day befriending those who oppose GOD and His messenger, even if they were their parents, or their children, or their siblings, or their tribe. For these, He decrees faith into their hearts, and supports them with inspiration from Him, and admits them into gardens with flowing streams wherein they abide forever. GOD is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. These are the party of GOD. Most assuredly, GOD's party are the winners.
58:22.arabic: لا تجد قوما يؤمنون بالله واليوم الأخر يوادون من حاد الله ورسوله ولو كانوا ءاباءهم أو أبناءهم أو إخونهم أو عشيرتهم أولـئك كتب في قلوبهم الإيمن وأيدهم بروح منه ويدخلهم جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر خلدين فيها رضي الله عنهم ورضوا عنه أولـئك حزب الله ألا إن حزب الله هم المفلحون

question: Who were the people of Sulaiman and what was their fate?
answer:
Solomon (Sulaymān) did not have a "people" in the destroyed-community sense of `Aad or Thamoud. He *ruled* people, *commanded* people, and *encountered* people. The question is best answered as: *over what communities did Solomon's authority extend, and what was their fate?*

**1. The inherited kingdom — Banī Isrā'īl under David's heir.**

Solomon inherited David's kingdom: [38:30]: "To David we granted Solomon; a good and obedient servant." Solomon's primary subjects were the Children of Israel — the same Israelites David had organized under prophet-kingship ([2:251]).

[27:15]: "We endowed David and Solomon with knowledge, and they said, 'Praise GOD for blessing us more than many of His believing servants.'" — the joint endowment of David and Solomon over the *same constituency*.

[21:79] notes the joint-judgment of David and Solomon over the *crop-and-sheep* dispute — both judges over the *same population*.

**2. The expanded retinue — humans, jinns, birds.**

Solomon's people were not just Israelites. The Quran preserves the *unique three-domain command*:

[27:17]: "Mobilized in the service of Solomon were his obedient soldiers of jinns and humans, as well as the birds; all at his disposal." — *junūduhū mina l-jinni wa-l-insi wa-ṭ-ṭayri*. The *people of Solomon* in the broad sense include: (a) human soldiers and citizens, (b) jinns subjected to his command, (c) birds in his communication network.

**3. The ant-colony.**

[27:18]: "When they approached the valley of the ants, one ant said, 'O you ants, go into your homes, lest you get crushed by Solomon and his soldiers, without perceiving.'" — the ants are *not* his subjects but a population through whose territory he passed. The verse preserves an ant's *recognition of authority without coercion*: she does not say *go to war*, she says *go home*. The fate of the ants: *not destroyed*; Solomon *smiles* at the warning and prays in [27:19].

**4. The Queen of Sheba's people.**

The Yemeni / Saba'i people under their queen were *not* Solomon's subjects initially. After his diplomatic invitation, throne-test, and the queen's conversion at the crystal floor ([27:44]), her *submission with Solomon to God* is described — but the surah does not say whether her *people* converted. Classical tradition adds biographical detail; the Quran is silent on the Sabaean people's mass conversion.

**5. The fate of Solomon's primary subjects after his death.**

[34:14]: "When the appointed time for his death came, nothing indicated to them that he had died until an earthworm ate (his staff). When he fell down, the jinns realized that if they really knew the unseen, they would have stopped working for Solomon. They would not have continued in such a humiliating retribution." — Solomon's *immediate post-mortem*: the jinns, no longer compelled, were freed from their forced labor.

Then surah 34 records a *historical disaster* that fell on the people of Saba' — though the connection to Solomon's reign in the chronology is indirect:

[34:15]–[34:17]: "The people of Sheba used to have a wonder in their homeland: two gardens, on the right and the left. Eat from your Lord's provisions, and be appreciative of Him — good land, and a forgiving Lord. They turned away. Consequently, we poured upon them a disastrous flood, and we substituted their two gardens into two gardens of bad-tasting fruits, thistles, and a skimpy harvest. We thus requited them for their disbelief. Do we requite except the disbelievers?" — the *flood of `Arim* destroyed the Sabaean prosperity. The text marks this as *requital for ingratitude* — the failure of Sheba's people to live up to the *prosperity-and-gratitude* contract.

**6. The bigger Israelite story.**

The Children of Israel under Solomon entered their golden age — the kingdom at its widest extent and most prosperous. Their *long-term fate* is the recurring Quranic theme: *blessed at first, divided, judged by the prophets*, with the verdict pronounced *by the tongue of David and Jesus* ([5:78]).

**Summary.**

Solomon's "people" — depending on the sense of the term — include: the Children of Israel he inherited from David; the humans, jinns, and birds *in his command*; the ants whose territory he respected; and the Queen of Sheba whose conversion is reported but whose people's response is *not*. The fate of his primary kingdom is *long-term mixed* — the Children of Israel pass through prosperity to division to verdict; the Sabaean prosperity collapses in the *flood of `Arim* ([34:16]); the jinns are freed from forced labor at Solomon's death ([34:14]). The pattern is *what happens when prophetic stewardship lapses* — most of the human dependence on the prophet-king's presence becomes visible only by its *absence*.

2:251.text: They defeated them by GOD's leave, and David killed Goliath. GOD gave him kingship and wisdom, and taught him as He willed. If it were not for GOD's support of some people against others, there would be chaos on earth. But GOD showers His grace upon the people.
2:251.arabic: فهزموهم بإذن الله وقتل داود جالوت وءاتىه الله الملك والحكمة وعلمه مما يشاء ولولا دفع الله الناس بعضهم ببعض لفسدت الأرض ولكن الله ذو فضل على العلمين

21:78.text: And David and Solomon, when they once ruled with regard to someone's crop that was destroyed by another's sheep, we witnessed their judgment.
21:78.arabic: وداود وسليمن إذ يحكمان في الحرث إذ نفشت فيه غنم القوم وكنا لحكمهم شهدين

21:79.text: We granted Solomon the correct understanding, though we endowed both of them with wisdom and knowledge. We committed the mountains to serve David in glorifying (God), as well as the birds. This is what we did.
21:79.arabic: ففهمنها سليمن وكلا ءاتينا حكما وعلما وسخرنا مع داود الجبال يسبحن والطير وكنا فعلين

21:81.text: For Solomon, we committed the wind gusting and blowing at his disposal. He could direct it as he wished, to whatever land he chose, and we blessed such land for him. We are fully aware of all things.
21:81.arabic: ولسليمن الريح عاصفة تجري بأمره إلى الأرض التي بركنا فيها وكنا بكل شيء علمين

27:15.text: We endowed David and Solomon with knowledge, and they said, "Praise GOD for blessing us more than many of His believing servants."
27:15.arabic: ولقد ءاتينا داود وسليمن علما وقالا الحمد لله الذي فضلنا على كثير من عباده المؤمنين

27:17.text: Mobilized in the service of Solomon were his obedient soldiers of jinns and humans, as well as the birds; all at his disposal.
27:17.arabic: وحشر لسليمن جنوده من الجن والإنس والطير فهم يوزعون

27:18.text: When they approached the valley of the ants, one ant said, "O you ants, go into your homes, lest you get crushed by Solomon and his soldiers, without perceiving."
27:18.arabic: حتى إذا أتوا على واد النمل قالت نملة يـأيها النمل ادخلوا مسكنكم لا يحطمنكم سليمن وجنوده وهم لا يشعرون

27:24.text: "I found her and her people prostrating before the sun, instead of GOD. The devil has adorned their works in their eyes, and has repulsed them from the path; consequently, they are not guided."
27:24.arabic: وجدتها وقومها يسجدون للشمس من دون الله وزين لهم الشيطن أعملهم فصدهم عن السبيل فهم لا يهتدون

27:44.text: She was told, "Go inside the palace." When she saw its interior, she thought it was a pool of water, and she (pulled up her dress,) exposing her legs. He said, "This interior is now paved with crystal." She said, "My Lord, I have wronged my soul. I now submit with Solomon to GOD, Lord of the universe."
27:44.arabic: قيل لها ادخلي الصرح فلما رأته حسبته لجة وكشفت عن ساقيها قال إنه صرح ممرد من قوارير قالت رب إني ظلمت نفسي وأسلمت مع سليمن لله رب العلمين

34:12.text: To Solomon we committed the wind at his disposal, traveling one month coming and one month going. And we caused a spring of oil to gush out for him. Also, the jinns worked for him, by his Lord's leave. Any one of them who disregarded our commands, we subjected him to a severe retribution.
34:12.arabic: ولسليمن الريح غدوها شهر ورواحها شهر وأسلنا له عين القطر ومن الجن من يعمل بين يديه بإذن ربه ومن يزغ منهم عن أمرنا نذقه من عذاب السعير

34:13.text: They made for him anything he wanted - niches, statues, deep pools, and heavy cooking pots. O family of David, work (righteousness) to show your appreciation. Only a few of My servants are appreciative. Jinns' Knowledge is Limited

34:14.text: When the appointed time for his death came, they had no clue that he had died. 34:16.arabic: فأعرضوا فأرسلنا عليهم سيل العرم وبدلنهم بجنتيهم جنتين ذواتي أكل خمط وأثل وشيء من سدر قليل

34:17.arabic: ذلك جزينهم بما كفروا وهل نجزي إلا الكفور

38:30.text: To David we granted Solomon; a good and obedient servant.
38:30.arabic: ووهبنا لداود سليمن نعم العبد إنه أواب

question: How is Sulaiman's relationship with God portrayed in the Quran?
answer:
Solomon's relationship with God in the Quran is constituted by four overlapping postures: (1) recipient of *unprecedented* divine gifts, (2) public attributor (he *says* the blessing is from God), (3) tested-by-wealth servant, and (4) prophet whose *post-mortem reputation* God Himself defends.

**1. Gifted with the unprecedented.**

[38:30]: "To David we granted Solomon; a good and obedient servant." — *ni`ma l-`abdu innahū awwāb*. Solomon enters the Quran as a *gift to David*, with the same epithet (*awwāb* — ever-returning) as his father.

[27:15]: "We endowed David and Solomon with knowledge." — *`ilm* is given.

[27:16]: "Solomon was David's heir. He said, 'O people, we have been endowed with understanding the language of the birds, and all kinds of things have been bestowed upon us.'" — the *language of the birds* (*manṭiq aṭ-ṭayr*) — unique among prophets.

[21:79]: "We granted Solomon the correct understanding..." — *correct judgment* in the crop-and-sheep dispute.

[21:81]: "For Solomon, we committed the wind gusting and blowing at his disposal." — *controlled wind*.

[34:12]: "To Solomon we committed the wind at his disposal, traveling one month coming and one month going. And we caused a spring of oil to gush out for him. Also, the jinns worked for him." — wind-range of one month each way; a *spring of molten copper / oil* gushing for him; jinns laboring.

[27:17]: "Mobilized in the service of Solomon were his obedient soldiers of jinns and humans, as well as the birds." — three-domain command.

**2. The verbal attribution.**

Solomon's relationship with God is repeatedly verbal — he *names* God as the source:

[27:16]: "This is indeed a real blessing." — *inna hādhā la-huwa l-faḍlu l-mubīn*.

[27:19] (after smiling at the ant): "My Lord, direct me to be appreciative of the blessings You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do the righteous works that please You, and admit me by Your mercy among Your righteous servants." — Solomon's *prayer in response to the ant* is *not pride at understanding her*, but *humility before the gift*.

[27:40]: "When Solomon saw it (the throne) placed in front of him, he said, 'This is a blessing from my Lord, whereby He tests me, to show whether I am appreciative or unappreciative. Whoever is appreciative is appreciative for his own good, and if one turns unappreciative, then my Lord is in no need for him, Most Honorable.'" — Solomon's *response to receiving the throne* is *self-reflective gratitude-or-the-failure-of-gratitude*.

[27:30]: His letter to the Queen of Sheba opens with *"In the name of GOD, Most Gracious, Most Merciful"* — the *basmalah*. Solomon's *diplomatic correspondence* opens with the divine name.

**3. Tested by wealth — and steadfast.**

[38:34]: "We thus put Solomon to the test; we blessed him with vast material wealth, but he steadfastly submitted." — *fatannā Sulaymān*. The unique formulation: *wealth as the test*. David's test was the two-litigants (a deprivation-of-justice scenario); Solomon's test is *the vast endowment itself*. The next verse:

[38:35]: "He said, 'My Lord, forgive me, and grant me a kingdom never attained by anyone else. You are the Grantor.'" — Solomon's *prayer at the moment of testing*: *forgive me* + *grant me an unprecedented kingdom*. Khalifa's translation preserves the *double-petition*; classical exegesis varies on whether the *unprecedented kingdom* request is itself part of the test he passed or part of his asking.

**4. The post-mortem disclosure — limits on the unseen.**

[34:14]: "When the appointed time for his death came, nothing indicated to them that he had died until an earthworm ate (his staff). When he fell down, the jinns realized that if they really knew the unseen, they would have stopped working for Solomon." — Solomon's *death moment* is also a *theological correction*: even Solomon does not arrange his own death to be visible; the jinns did not know the unseen, and Solomon's *post-mortem standing prop* exposes this. His relationship with God includes *being used to correct jinn folk-theology after his death*.

**5. The defended reputation — against the post-mortem sorcery slander.**

[2:102]: "They pursued what the devils taught concerning Solomon's kingdom. Solomon, however, was not a disbeliever, but the devils were disbelievers. They taught the people sorcery, and that which was sent down through the two angels of Babel, Hārūt and Mārūt. These two never teach anyone without first declaring, 'This is only a test; do not abandon your faith.' People learn from them how to (use sorcery to) create discord between husband and wife. They can never hurt anyone except by GOD's will." — the Quran explicitly *defends* Solomon against the *post-mortem sorcery slander*. *Solomon was not a disbeliever; the devils were the disbelievers.* The relationship with God includes God's own *vindication of Solomon's name* against the magical-Solomon traditions that grew up in the Mesopotamian and later European demonological literature.

**6. The judgment-witness.**

[21:78]: "And David and Solomon, when they once ruled with regard to someone's crop that was destroyed by another's sheep, we witnessed their judgment." — *we witnessed*. The relationship includes *God's presence at his court*.

**7. The signature epithet.**

[38:30]: *ni`ma l-`abdu innahū awwāb*. The same *awwāb* applied to David is applied to Solomon — *ever-returning to God*. The relationship is one of *constant return*, not of *one-time conversion*.

**Synthesis.**

Solomon is given *the unprecedented*: bird-language, wind-control, jinn-labor, ant-comprehension, the molten copper spring, the knowledgeable retriever of the throne. His *first response* in every case is *verbal attribution* — *this is from my Lord*. He is *tested by wealth*, the inverse of the deprivation-test, and *passes by submission* ([38:34]). His death is *a theological correction* of jinn-omniscience claims ([34:14]). His *post-mortem reputation* is *defended by God Himself* against the sorcery-slander ([2:102]). His relationship is one of *attributing-to-God under unprecedented endowment* — gratitude as the structural response to receipt of *what no one had before*.

2:102.text: They pursued what the devils taught concerning Solomon's kingdom. Solomon, however, was not a disbeliever, but the devils were disbelievers. They taught the people sorcery, and that which was sent down through the two angels of Babel, Haroot and Maroot. These two did not divulge such knowledge without pointing out: "This is a test. You shall not abuse such knowledge." But the people used it in such evil schemes as the breaking up of marriages. They can never harm anyone against the will of GOD. They thus learn what hurts them, not what benefits them, and they know full well that whoever practices witchcraft will have no share in the Hereafter. Miserable indeed is what they sell their souls for, if they only knew.
2:102.arabic: واتبعوا ما تتلوا الشيطين على ملك سليمن وما كفر سليمن ولكن الشيطين كفروا يعلمون الناس السحر وما أنزل على الملكين ببابل هروت ومروت وما يعلمان من أحد حتى يقولا إنما نحن فتنة فلا تكفر فيتعلمون منهما ما يفرقون به بين المرء وزوجه وما هم بضارين به من أحد إلا بإذن الله ويتعلمون ما يضرهم ولا ينفعهم ولقد علموا لمن اشترىه ما له في الأخرة من خلق ولبئس ما شروا به أنفسهم لو كانوا يعلمون

21:78.text: And David and Solomon, when they once ruled with regard to someone's crop that was destroyed by another's sheep, we witnessed their judgment.
21:78.arabic: وداود وسليمن إذ يحكمان في الحرث إذ نفشت فيه غنم القوم وكنا لحكمهم شهدين

21:81.arabic: ولسليمن الريح عاصفة تجري بأمره إلى الأرض التي بركنا فيها وكنا بكل شيء علمين

Whoever is appreciative is appreciative for his own good, and if one turns unappreciative, then my Lord is in no need for him, Most Honorable."
27:40.arabic: قال الذي عنده علم من الكتب أنا ءاتيك به قبل أن يرتد إليك طرفك فلما رءاه مستقرا عنده قال هذا من فضل ربي ليبلوني ءأشكر أم أكفر ومن شكر فإنما يشكر لنفسه ومن كفر فإن ربي غني كريم

38:35.arabic: قال رب اغفر لي وهب لي ملكا لا ينبغي لأحد من بعدي إنك أنت الوهاب

38:40.text: He has deserved an honorable position with us, and a wonderful abode.
38:40.arabic: وإن له عندنا لزلفى وحسن مـاب

question: What is the central message of Surah 64 (Mutual Blaming)?
answer:
Surah 64 (At-Taghābun — "Mutual Blaming") is eighteen verses, Medinan. The surah's title comes from [64:9], which names the Day of Resurrection as *yawmu t-Taghābun* — "the Day of Mutual Blaming" or, more precisely, *the day of mutual dispossession*. The central message: *every reality on this earth — your spouse, your child, your money, your hardship — is a test*; the *successful* are those who *recognize this in time*.

**Universal glorification.**

[64:1]: "Glorifying GOD is everything in the heavens and everything on earth. To Him belongs all kingship, and to Him belongs all praise, and He is Omnipotent." — *yusabbiḥu li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ, lahu l-mulku wa-lahu l-ḥamd*. Sovereignty and praise *both belong to Him*. The third *musabbiḥāt* opening.

**The two basic categories.**

[64:2]: "He is the One who created you, then among you there is the disbeliever, and the believer. GOD is fully Seer of everything you do." — *fa-minkum kāfirun wa-minkum mu'min*. From the *same creative act* come *two outcomes*. The verse refuses the predestination-without-agency reading: *God created you*, and *among you* belief and disbelief are *both present*.

**Purposive creation.**

[64:3]: "He created the heavens and the earth for a specific purpose, designed you and perfected your design, then to Him is the final destiny." — *khalaqakum fa-aḥsana ṣuwarakum*. *He shaped you and perfected your shapes.*

[64:4]: "He knows everything in the heavens and the earth, and He knows everything you conceal and everything you declare. GOD is fully aware of the innermost thoughts." — *bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūr* — *of what is in the chests*. Innermost knowledge.

**The historical instruction.**

[64:5]–[64:6]: "Have you noted those who disbelieved in the past, then suffered the consequences of their decision? They incurred a painful retribution. This is because their messengers went to them with clear proofs, but they said, 'Shall we follow humans like us?' They disbelieved and turned away. GOD does not need them; GOD is in no need, Praiseworthy." — *a-basharun yahdūnanā* — *"shall humans guide us?"* — the disbelievers' standing objection. The Quranic answer is implicit: *yes, humans guide; that is the prophetic mechanism*.

**The resurrection oath.**

[64:7]: "Those who disbelieved claim that they will not be resurrected! Yes indeed, by my Lord, you will be resurrected, and you will be held accountable for everything you have done. This is easy for GOD to do." — *qul balā wa-rabbī la-tub`athunna thumma la-tunabba'unna bi-mā `amiltum*. The Quran has the Prophet *swear by his Lord*: *you will be resurrected*.

**The light revealed.**

[64:8]: "Therefore, you shall believe in GOD and His messenger, and the light that we have revealed herein. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do." — *an-nūru lladhī anzalnā* — *the light We have sent down*. The Quran is named as *light*.

**The Day named.**

[64:9]: "The day will come when He summons you to the Day of Summoning. That is the Day of Mutual Blaming. Anyone who believes in GOD and leads a righteous life, He will remit his sins, and will admit him into gardens with flowing streams. They abide therein forever. This is the greatest triumph." — *yawma yajma`ukum li-yawmi l-jam`i dhālika yawmu t-Taghābun*. *Yawmu l-Taghābun* — the day when *each side feels deprived/blamed in relation to the other*: the believer realizes how *little* he could have done compared to the reward; the disbeliever realizes how *much* he forfeited compared to what was available. The *mutual dispossession* cuts both ways.

**The affliction-as-test.**

[64:11]: "Nothing happens to you except in accordance with GOD's will. Anyone who believes in GOD, He will guide his heart. GOD is fully aware of all things." — *mā aṣāba min muṣībatin illā bi-idhni llāh*. Affliction is *not random*; it occurs *by God's leave*; and the believer's *heart is guided* (*yahdi qalbah*) in the wake of affliction.

**The spouse-child-money triad.**

[64:14]: "O you who believe, your spouses and your children can be your enemies; beware. If you pardon, forget, and forgive, then GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *min azwājikum wa-awlādikum `aduwwun lakum*. The verse identifies a *category* — *some of your spouses and children are an enemy to you* — and asks for *pardon-and-forgiveness*, not retaliation. The verse is among the Quran's most realistic about *intra-family religious tension*.

[64:15]: "Your money and children are a test, and GOD possesses a great recompense." — *innamā amwālukum wa-awlādukum fitnah*. *Wealth and children are a test*. Two of the most-loved categories of human attainment are explicitly *flagged as fitnah*.

**The triple instruction.**

[64:16]: "Therefore, you shall reverence GOD as much as you can, and listen, and obey, and give (to charity) for your own good. Anyone who is protected from his own stinginess, these are the successful ones." — *fa-ttaqū llāha mā staṭa`tum*. *Reverence God as much as you can*. The verse is one of the Quran's most pastoral statements: *the requirement is calibrated to capacity*. Then: *listen, obey, give*. And the closing diagnostic: *whoever is protected from his own stinginess (shuḥḥ nafsih) is among the successful*. *Internal stinginess* (not external) is the *salvation-blocker*.

**The loan-to-God formulation.**

[64:17]: "If you lend GOD a loan of righteousness, He will multiply the reward for you manifold, and forgive you. GOD is Appreciative, Clement." — *qarḍan ḥasanan*. *Lending God a good loan* — the Quranic metaphor for charity. The verse closes with *shakūrun ḥalīm* — *Appreciative, Clement* — God's response is *thankful*.

**Central message in one sentence.**

You will be resurrected ([64:7]); the Day is *Mutual Blaming* — both sides feel dispossessed in relation to what was available ([64:9]); your spouse, your child, your money, your affliction are *all tests* ([64:11], [64:14]–[64:15]); *reverence God as much as you can* — calibrate to capacity ([64:16]); be *protected from your own stinginess*, and *lend God a good loan* — the *successful* are those who recognize all of this *in time*.

64:1.text: Glorifying GOD is everything in the heavens and everything on earth. To Him belongs all kingship, and to Him belongs all praise, and He is Omnipotent.
64:1.arabic: يسبح لله ما في السموت وما في الأرض له الملك وله الحمد وهو على كل شيء قدير

64:2.text: He is the One who created you, then among you there is the disbeliever, and the believer. GOD is fully Seer of everything you do.
64:2.arabic: هو الذي خلقكم فمنكم كافر ومنكم مؤمن والله بما تعملون بصير

64:3.text: He created the heavens and the earth for a specific purpose, designed you and perfected your design, then to Him is the final destiny.
64:3.arabic: خلق السموت والأرض بالحق وصوركم فأحسن صوركم وإليه المصير

64:4.text: He knows everything in the heavens and the earth, and He knows everything you conceal and everything you declare. GOD is fully aware of the innermost thoughts.
64:4.arabic: يعلم ما في السموت والأرض ويعلم ما تسرون وما تعلنون والله عليم بذات الصدور

64:5.text: Have you noted those who disbelieved in the past, then suffered the consequences of their decision? They incurred a painful retribution.
64:5.arabic: ألم يأتكم نبؤا الذين كفروا من قبل فذاقوا وبال أمرهم ولهم عذاب أليم

64:7.text: Those who disbelieved claim that they will not be resurrected! Yes indeed, by my Lord, you will be resurrected, and you will be held accountable for everything you have done. This is easy for GOD to do.
64:7.arabic: زعم الذين كفروا أن لن يبعثوا قل بلى وربي لتبعثن ثم لتنبؤن بما عملتم وذلك على الله يسير

64:8.text: Therefore, you shall believe in GOD and His messenger, and the light that we have revealed herein. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
64:8.arabic: فـامنوا بالله ورسوله والنور الذي أنزلنا والله بما تعملون خبير

64:9.text: The day will come when He summons you to the Day of Summoning. That is the Day of Mutual Blaming. Anyone who believes in GOD and leads a righteous life, He will remit his sins, and will admit him into gardens with flowing streams. They abide therein forever. This is the greatest triumph.
64:9.arabic: يوم يجمعكم ليوم الجمع ذلك يوم التغابن ومن يؤمن بالله ويعمل صلحا يكفر عنه سيـاته ويدخله جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر خلدين فيها أبدا ذلك الفوز العظيم

64:11.text: Nothing happens to you except in accordance with GOD's will. Anyone who believes in GOD, He will guide his heart. GOD is fully aware of all things.
64:11.arabic: ما أصاب من مصيبة إلا بإذن الله ومن يؤمن بالله يهد قلبه والله بكل شيء عليم

64:14.text: O you who believe, your spouses and your children can be your enemies; beware. If you pardon, forget, and forgive, then GOD is Forgiver, Most Merciful.
64:14.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إن من أزوجكم وأولدكم عدوا لكم فاحذروهم وإن تعفوا وتصفحوا وتغفروا فإن الله غفور رحيم

64:15.text: Your money and children are a test, and GOD possesses a great recompense.
64:15.arabic: إنما أمولكم وأولدكم فتنة والله عنده أجر عظيم

64:16.text: Therefore, you shall reverence GOD as much as you can, and listen, and obey, and give (to charity) for your own good. Anyone who is protected from his own stinginess, these are the successful ones.
64:16.arabic: فاتقوا الله ما استطعتم واسمعوا وأطيعوا وأنفقوا خيرا لأنفسكم ومن يوق شح نفسه فأولـئك هم المفلحون

64:17.text: If you lend GOD a loan of righteousness, He will multiply the reward for you manifold, and forgive you. GOD is Appreciative, Clement.
64:17.arabic: إن تقرضوا الله قرضا حسنا يضعفه لكم ويغفر لكم والله شكور حليم

question: What is the central message of Surah 51 (Drivers of the Winds)?
answer:
Surah 51 (Adh-Dhāriyāt — "Drivers of the Winds" / "The Scatterers") is sixty verses, Meccan. The surah opens with a four-fold oath sequence about wind-cycles, traverses a series of historical destruction-narratives (Abraham's visitors, Lot's town, Moses-Pharaoh, `Aad, Thamoud, Noah), and closes with what may be the Quran's single most condensed statement of human purpose: *I did not create the jinns and the humans except to worship Me alone* ([51:56]).

**The four oaths — the wind-cycle.**

[51:1]: "The blowing winds." — *wa-dh-dhāriyāti dharwā*. *Dharā* = to scatter, to winnow.

[51:2]: "Bearing rain." — *fa-l-ḥāmilāti wiqrā*. *Wiqr* = a heavy burden — clouds *bearing* their rain-weight.

[51:3]: "Bringing provisions." — *fa-l-jāriyāti yusrā*. *Jāriyāt* — "moving / running" — usually understood as ships moving with ease (or, in some readings, clouds).

[51:4]: "Distributing them as commanded." — *fa-l-muqassimāti amrā*. *The distributors* — the angels or natural processes that distribute according to command.

The four oaths together: *winnowing → bearing → moving → distributing*. Four stages of a single cycle (evaporation → cloud-formation → transit → distribution). The cycle is itself the *witness*.

**The verdict.**

[51:5]–[51:6]: "What is promised to you will surely come to pass. The Day of Judgment is inevitable." — *innamā tū`adūna la-ṣādiq, wa-inna d-dīna la-wāqi`*.

**The pious — what they actually do.**

[51:15]–[51:19] give a tight portrait of *al-muttaqūn* (the reverent):

[51:15]: "The righteous have deserved gardens and springs."

[51:17]: "Rarely did they sleep the whole night." — *kānū qalīlan mina l-layli mā yahja`ūn*. *Tahajjud* — night-prayer.

[51:18]: "At dawn, they prayed for forgiveness." — *wa-bi-l-asḥāri hum yastaghfirūn*.

[51:19]: "A portion of their money was set aside for the beggar and the needy." — *wa-fī amwālihim ḥaqqun li-s-sā'ili wa-l-maḥrūm*. *A right* (not a favor) *for the beggar and the deprived*.

The portrait condenses to three operations: *night vigil, dawn-istighfar, share-of-wealth for the asker and the deprived*.

**The signs — outer and inner.**

[51:20]: "The earth is full of signs for those who are certain."

[51:21]: "And within yourselves; can you see?" — *wa-fī anfusikum afa-lā tubṣirūn*. *In your selves* — anatomy, psychology, conscience — are signs.

[51:22]: "In the heaven is your provision, and everything that is promised to you." — provision and promise are *up* (the sky), not just laterally distributed.

**Abraham's guests (51:24–37).**

[51:24]–[51:37]: The angelic messengers visit Abraham, announce Isaac's birth to elderly Sarah, then proceed to Lot's town. Abraham asks them their mission; they identify it: *to a criminal people*. The destruction of Lot's town follows, *except one house of submitters* ([51:36]).

**Moses, `Aad, Thamoud, Noah (51:38–46).**

A rapid roster of *historical analogies*:

[51:38]–[51:40]: Moses → Pharaoh → drowning.

[51:41]–[51:42]: `Aad → the disastrous wind → utter destruction.

[51:43]–[51:45]: Thamoud → the camel → lightning.

[51:46]: Noah's people, before all of them.

**The expanding heaven.**

[51:47]: "We constructed the sky with our hands, and we will continue to expand it." — *wa-s-samā'a banaynāhā bi-aydin wa-innā la-mūsi`ūn*. *Mūsi`ūn* — "expanding ones." Khalifa annotates this verse as one of the Quran's "scientific foreshadowings" — the *expansion of the universe*. The classical reading is *spacious / capacious*; Khalifa emphasizes the active-participle *we are continually expanding*.

[51:48]: "And we made the earth habitable; a perfect design."

[51:49]: "We created a pair (male and female) of everything, that you may take heed." — *wa-min kulli shay'in khalaqnā zawjayn*. *Of every thing we created a pair*.

**The instruction.**

[51:50]: "You shall escape to GOD. I am sent by Him to you as a manifest warner." — *fa-firrū ilā llāh*. *Flee to God* — escape *toward* God, not from Him.

[51:51]: "Do not set up beside GOD any other god. I am sent by Him to you as a manifest warner."

**The single-line statement of human purpose.**

[51:56]: "I did not create the jinns and the humans except to worship Me alone." — *wa-mā khalaqtu l-jinna wa-l-insa illā li-ya`budūn*. The most-condensed Quranic statement of *the human telos*. The verse identifies *worship* (*`ibādah*) as the *exclusive purpose* of creation for the two species of moral agents (jinn and human).

**God needs nothing.**

[51:57]: "I need no provisions from them, nor do I need them to feed Me." — *mā urīdu minhum min rizq, wa-mā urīdu an yuṭ`imūn*. The corollary of the worship-purpose: God's need-of-worship is *not* a need of *what worshipers can provide*. He does not need provisions or food. The worship-requirement is *for the worshipers*, not for Him.

[51:58]: "GOD is the Provider, the Possessor of all power, the Supreme." — *inna llāha huwa r-Razzāqu dhū l-quwwati l-matīn*. The closing inversion: *He is the Provider*; provision flows from Him, not toward Him.

**Central message.**

Wind-cycles testify ([51:1]–[51:4]); the Day is inevitable ([51:6]); the pious *vigil by night, ask forgiveness at dawn, share their wealth with the asker and the deprived* ([51:15]–[51:19]); the signs are *outside and inside* ([51:20]–[51:21]); the historical roster of destruction (Lot, Moses-Pharaoh, `Aad, Thamoud, Noah) is the *witness-list of warning*; the heaven is *expanding* ([51:47]); creation came *in pairs* ([51:49]); and the *single irreducible reason for human existence* is *worship of God alone* — He needs nothing from you; the requirement is *for you* ([51:56]–[51:58]).

51:5.text: What is promised to you will surely come to pass.

51:6.text: The Day of Judgment is inevitable.

51:15.text: The righteous have deserved gardens and springs.

51:17.text: Rarely did they sleep the whole night.
51:17.arabic: كانوا قليلا من اليل ما يهجعون

51:18.text: At dawn, they prayed for forgiveness.

51:19.text: A portion of their money was set aside for the beggar and the needy.

51:20.text: The earth is full of signs for those who are certain.

51:21.text: And within yourselves; can you see?

51:22.text: In the heaven is your provision, and everything that is promised to you.

51:24.text: Have you noted the history of Abraham's honorable guests?
51:24.arabic: هل أتىك حديث ضيف إبرهيم المكرمين

51:36.text: We did not find in it except one house of submitters.
51:36.arabic: فما وجدنا فيها غير بيت من المسلمين

51:38.text: In Moses (there is a lesson). We sent him to Pharaoh with manifest proofs.
51:38.arabic: وفي موسى إذ أرسلنه إلى فرعون بسلطن مبين

51:41.text: In `Aad (there is a lesson). We sent upon them disastrous wind.
51:41.arabic: وفي عاد إذ أرسلنا عليهم الريح العقيم

51:43.text: temporarily." In Thamoud (there is a lesson). They were told, "Enjoy
51:43.arabic: وفي ثمود إذ قيل لهم تمتعوا حتى حين

51:47.text: We constructed the sky with our hands, and we will continue to expand it.

51:49.text: We created a pair (male and female) of everything, that you may take heed.
51:49.arabic: ومن كل شيء خلقنا زوجين لعلكم تذكرون

51:50.text: You shall escape to GOD. I am sent by Him to you as a manifest warner.
51:50.arabic: ففروا إلى الله إني لكم منه نذير مبين

51:56.text: I did not create the jinns and the humans except to worship Me alone.
51:56.arabic: وما خلقت الجن والإنس إلا ليعبدون

51:57.text: I need no provisions from them, nor do I need them to feed Me.
51:57.arabic: ما أريد منهم من رزق وما أريد أن يطعمون

51:58.text: GOD is the Provider, the Possessor of all power, the Supreme.
51:58.arabic: إن الله هو الرزاق ذو القوة المتين

question: What is the central message of Surah 53 (The Stars)?
answer:
Surah 53 (An-Najm — "The Star") is sixty-two verses, Meccan. The surah carries an unusually personal weight: it preserves the *first-person testimony* of Muhammad's ascent-vision and is the surah that triggered the first public mass-prostration when he recited it before the Quraysh. Its central message is the *defense of revelation as actual seeing* (the Prophet's vision is not invention), the *demolition of the female-deity claim* of pre-Islamic Mecca, and the *salvific minimum*: *no soul carries another's burden, the human gets only what he strove for*.

**Opening — the Prophet is not astray.**

[53:1]: "As the stars fell away." — *wa-n-najmi idhā hawā*. The oath is *on the falling star* — *najm* (star) and *hawā* (to fall, descend) — possibly referring to the *meteor shower phenomenon* or, in some classical readings, the *Pleiades descending below the horizon*.

[53:2]: "Your friend (Muhammad) was not astray, nor was he deceived." — *mā ḍalla ṣāḥibukum wa-mā ghawā*. *Your friend* — the friend-relation, *ṣāḥib*, asserts intimate familiarity. The Quraysh knew Muhammad as *al-Amīn* (the trustworthy) before his prophethood; the verse asserts the *continuity* of that character into his revelations.

[53:3]: "Nor was he speaking out of a personal desire." — *wa-mā yanṭiqu `ani l-hawā*. *Hawā* — personal inclination / passion. The Prophet's speech is *not* from *hawā*.

[53:4]: "It was divine inspiration." — *in huwa illā waḥyun yūḥā*. *Waḥy* — inspiration.

**The vision sequence.**

[53:5]–[53:11]: The being who taught him — *strongest in power* (*shadīdu l-quwā*), *possessor of authority* (*dhū mirrah*), *settled at the highest horizon* (*bi-l-ufuqi l-a`lā*), *drew near and descended* (*danā fa-tadallā*), *until he became as close as possible* (*qāba qawsayni aw adnā*) — *two bow-lengths or closer* — *He then revealed to His servant what was to be revealed*. The vision is described in language of *spatial intimacy*: *the messenger of revelation descends to within two bow-lengths of him*. The classical identification is Gabriel; some Sufi readings see the experience as direct.

[53:11]: "The mind never made up what it saw." — *mā kadhaba l-fu'ādu mā ra'ā*. *Fu'ād* — the heart-mind — *did not invent* what it saw.

**The second vision — the *Sidrat al-Muntahā*.**

[53:13]–[53:18]: "He saw him in another descent. At the ultimate point. Where the eternal Paradise is located. The whole place was overwhelmed. The eyes did not waver, nor go blind. He saw great signs of his Lord." — *`inda sidrati l-muntahā* — *at the lote-tree of the ultimate limit*. The location is the *boundary* of access. The eye *did not deviate* (*mā zāgha l-baṣar*) *nor transgress* (*wa-mā ṭaghā*).

**The demolition of the female deities.**

[53:19]–[53:23]: "Compare this with the female idols Allaat and Al-`Uzzah. And Manaat, the third one. Do you have sons, while He has these as daughters? What a disgraceful distribution! These are but names that you made up, you and your forefathers. GOD never authorized such a blasphemy. They follow conjecture, and personal desire, when the true guidance has come to them herein from their Lord."

The verse names *three specific deities of pre-Islamic Mecca* — al-Lāt, al-`Uzzā, Manāt — and *demolishes the assignment of feminine identity to them as God's daughters*.
