12:8.text: They said, "Joseph and his brother are favored by our father, and we are in the majority. Indeed, our father is far astray. Joseph's Fate Already Decided by God
12:8.arabic: إذ قالوا ليوسف وأخوه أحب إلى أبينا منا ونحن عصبة إن أبانا لفي ضلل مبين

12:10.text: One of them said, "Do not kill Joseph; let us throw him into the abyss of the well. Perhaps some caravan can pick him up, if this is what you decide to do."
12:10.arabic: قال قائل منهم لا تقتلوا يوسف وألقوه في غيبت الجب يلتقطه بعض السيارة إن كنتم فعلين

12:15.text: When they went away with him, and unanimously decided to throw him into the abyss of the well, we inspired him: "Some day, you will tell them about all this, while they have no idea."
12:15.arabic: فلما ذهبوا به وأجمعوا أن يجعلوه في غيبت الجب وأوحينا إليه لتنبئنهم بأمرهم هذا وهم لا يشعرون

12:19.text: A caravan passed by, and soon sent their waterer. He let down his bucket, then said, "How lucky! There is a boy here!" They took him along as merchandise, and GOD was fully aware of what they did.
12:19.arabic: وجاءت سيارة فأرسلوا واردهم فأدلى دلوه قال يبشرى هذا غلم وأسروه بضعة والله عليم بما يعملون

12:21.text: The one who bought him in Egypt said to his wife, "Take good care of him. Maybe he can help us, or maybe we can adopt him." We thus established Joseph on earth, and we taught him the interpretation of dreams. GOD's command is always done, but most people do not know.
12:21.arabic: وقال الذي اشترىه من مصر لامرأته أكرمي مثوىه عسى أن ينفعنا أو نتخذه ولدا وكذلك مكنا ليوسف في الأرض ولنعلمه من تأويل الأحاديث والله غالب على أمره ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون

12:30.text: Some women in the city gossiped: "The governor's wife is trying to seduce her servant. She is deeply in love with him. We see that she has gone astray."
12:30.arabic: ۞وقال نسوة في المدينة امرأت العزيز ترود فتىها عن نفسه قد شغفها حبا إنا لنرىها في ضلل مبين

12:55.text: He said, "Make me the treasurer, for I am experienced in this area and knowledgeable."
12:55.arabic: قال اجعلني على خزائن الأرض إني حفيظ عليم

12:58.text: Joseph's brothers came; when they entered, he recognized them, while they did not recognize him.
12:58.arabic: وجاء إخوة يوسف فدخلوا عليه فعرفهم وهم له منكرون

12:59.text: After he provided them with their provisions, he said, "Next time, bring with you your half-brother. Do you not see that I give full measure, and treat you generously?
12:59.arabic: ولما جهزهم بجهازهم قال ائتوني بأخ لكم من أبيكم ألا ترون أني أوفي الكيل وأنا خير المنزلين

12:78.text: They said, "O you noble one, he has a father who is elderly; would you take one of us in his place? We see that you are a kind man."
12:78.arabic: قالوا يـأيها العزيز إن له أبا شيخا كبيرا فخذ أحدنا مكانه إنا نرىك من المحسنين

12:89.text: He said, "Do you recall what you did to Joseph and his brother when you were ignorant?"
12:89.arabic: قال هل علمتم ما فعلتم بيوسف وأخيه إذ أنتم جهلون

12:91.text: They said, "By GOD, GOD has truly preferred you over us. We were definitely wrong."
12:91.arabic: قالوا تالله لقد ءاثرك الله علينا وإن كنا لخطـين

12:92.text: He said, "There is no blame upon you today. May GOD forgive you. Of all the merciful ones, He is the Most Merciful.
12:92.arabic: قال لا تثريب عليكم اليوم يغفر الله لكم وهو أرحم الرـحمين

12:99.text: When they entered Joseph's quarters, he embraced his parents, saying, "Welcome to Egypt. GOD willing, you will be safe here."
12:99.arabic: فلما دخلوا على يوسف ءاوى إليه أبويه وقال ادخلوا مصر إن شاء الله ءامنين

question: How is Yusuf's relationship with God portrayed in the Quran?
answer:
Yūsuf's relationship with God in the Quran is portrayed across four overlapping frames: (1) the *recipient of dreams and dream-interpretation*, (2) the *one whom God protects from the lapse*, (3) the *open public witness* of monotheism in prison, and (4) the *one who reads his own life-story as God's authorship*.

**1. Recipient of dreams and the interpretation-gift.**

[12:4]: "Recall that Joseph said to his father, 'O my father, I saw eleven planets, and the sun, and the moon; I saw them prostrating before me.'" — the *foundational dream*, given in childhood.

[12:37]: "He said, 'If any food is provided to you, I can inform you about it before you receive it. This is some of the knowledge bestowed upon me by my Lord.'" — Joseph in prison, identifying his interpretive gift as *God-given*. *Dhālikumā mimmā `allamanī rabbī* — *that is from what my Lord taught me*.

[12:101]: "My Lord, You have given me kingship and taught me the interpretation of dreams." — *`allamtanī min ta'wīli l-aḥādīth*. The verse names *dream-interpretation* as *one of two gifts* (kingship + interpretation) Joseph attributes to God in his closing prayer.

**2. Protected at the temptation moment.**

[12:23]: At the seduction attempt: "He said, 'May GOD protect me. He is my Lord, who gave me a good home. The transgressors never succeed.'" — *ma`ādha llāh*. Joseph's first response is *taking refuge in God*.

[12:24]: "She almost succumbed to him, and he almost succumbed to her, if it were not that he saw a proof from his Lord. We thus diverted evil and sin away from him, for he was one of our devoted servants." — *innahū min `ibādinā l-mukhlaṣīn*. *He was of Our devoted servants*. The Quran's *self-comment*: Joseph's protection from the lapse was God's act, not Joseph's autonomous strength. *We diverted evil and sin away from him.*

[12:33]: "He said, 'My Lord, the prison is better than giving in to them. Unless You divert their scheming from me, I may desire them and behave like the ignorant ones.'" — *the prayer for diversion*. Joseph asks God to *divert their scheming*.

[12:34]: "His Lord answered his prayer and diverted their scheming from him. He is the Hearer, the Omniscient." — the *answered prayer*.

**3. The prison witness — explicit monotheism.**

[12:38]: "I followed instead the religion of my ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. We never set up any idols beside GOD. Such is the blessing from GOD upon us and upon the people, but most people are unappreciative." — Joseph names *Abraham, Isaac, Jacob* as his line.

[12:39]–[12:40]: "O my prison mates, are several gods better, or GOD alone, the Supreme? You do not worship beside Him except innovations that you have made up, you and your parents. GOD has never authorized such idols. All ruling belongs to GOD, and He has ruled that you shall not worship except Him. This is the perfect religion, but most people do not know." — the *prison sermon*. Joseph turns dream-interpretation request into *direct monotheistic preaching*: *several gods or one*? *all ruling belongs to God*. The Quran preserves this as one of its tightest *creed-statements* delivered by any prophet.

**4. The diagnosis of the self.**

[12:53]: "I do not claim innocence for myself. The self is an advocate of evil, except for those who have attained mercy from my Lord. My Lord is Forgiver, Most Merciful." — *inna n-nafsa la-ammāratun bi-s-sū'i illā mā raḥima rabbī*. This is the foundational verse for *an-nafs al-ammārah* in Islamic moral psychology. Joseph's relationship with God includes *honest self-diagnosis*: he does not claim *immaculate* status, only *protected* status (by God's mercy).

**5. The non-retaliation at the moment of victory.**

[12:90]: "He said, 'GOD has blessed us. That is because if one leads a righteous life, and steadfastly perseveres, GOD never fails to reward the righteous.'" — *innahū man yattaqi wa-yaṣbir fa-inna llāha lā yuḍī`u ajra l-muḥsinīn*. The relationship with God is *causally connected to the long arc of righteous conduct*: *taqwā + ṣabr → unwasted reward*. Joseph reads his own restoration as *God's reward for piety + patience*.

**6. The patient grief — passed via father.**

[12:83]: Jacob's response when Benjamin is also lost: "He said, 'Indeed, your egos have created a certain scheme. Patience is my recourse. May GOD bring them all back to me. He is the Omniscient, Most Wise.'" — *fa-ṣabrun jamīlun*. *Beautiful patience.*

[12:86]: "I simply complain to GOD about my dilemma and grief, for I know from GOD what you do not know." — *I complain only to God*. Jacob's posture is preserved as *the model for Joseph's own household and for the Quran's readers*. Joseph's relationship with God *inherits* this posture from his father.

**7. The long-arc reading.**

[12:100]: "He said, 'O my father, this is the fulfillment of my old dream. My Lord has made it come true. He has blessed me, delivered me from the prison, and brought you from the desert, after the devil had driven a wedge between me and my brothers. My Lord is Most Kind towards whomever He wills. He is the Knower, the Most Wise.'" — *qad ja`alahā rabbī ḥaqqā*. *My Lord made it come true*. Joseph reads *his entire life-narrative* — the dream of childhood, the well, slavery, prison, ascent, the famine, the reunion — as *God's authorship*. The relationship is *retrospective recognition*: in the moment, the events were opaque; from the end, they are *God's design*.

**8. The closing prayer.**

[12:101]: "My Lord, You have given me kingship and taught me the interpretation of dreams. Initiator of the heavens and the earth; You are my Lord and Master in this life and in the Hereafter. Let me die as a submitter, and count me with the righteous." — *tawaffanī musliman wa-alḥiqnī bi-ṣ-ṣāliḥīn*. The closing prayer is *for death-as-submitter* and *joining the righteous*.

**Synthesis.**

Joseph's relationship with God is portrayed as: *receiver of dreams and interpretive gift* ([12:4], [12:37]); *protected from the lapse by divine diversion* ([12:24], [12:34]); *open witness of monotheism in prison* ([12:38]–[12:40]); *honest self-diagnoser of the soul's bent toward evil* ([12:53]); *non-retaliator at the moment of victory* ([12:90]); *inheritor of Jacob's beautiful patience* ([12:86]); *retrospective reader of his life as God's design* ([12:100]); and *closing prayer for death-as-submitter* ([12:101]). The portrait is *the just sufferer protected and restored*.

12:4.text: Recall that Joseph said to his father, "O my father, I saw eleven planets, and the sun, and the moon; I saw them prostrating before me."
12:4.arabic: إذ قال يوسف لأبيه يـأبت إني رأيت أحد عشر كوكبا والشمس والقمر رأيتهم لي سجدين

12:23.text: The lady of the house where he lived tried to seduce him. She closed the doors and said, "I am all yours." He said, "May GOD protect me. He is my Lord, who gave me a good home. The transgressors never succeed."
12:23.arabic: ورودته التي هو في بيتها عن نفسه وغلقت الأبوب وقالت هيت لك قال معاذ الله إنه ربي أحسن مثواي إنه لا يفلح الظـلمون

12:24.text: She almost succumbed to him, and he almost succumbed to her, if it were not that he saw a proof from his Lord. We thus diverted evil and sin away from him, for he was one of our devoted servants.
12:24.arabic: ولقد همت به وهم بها لولا أن رءا برهن ربه كذلك لنصرف عنه السوء والفحشاء إنه من عبادنا المخلصين

12:33.text: He said, "My Lord, the prison is better than giving in to them. Unless You divert their scheming from me, I may desire them and behave like the ignorant ones."
12:33.arabic: قال رب السجن أحب إلي مما يدعونني إليه وإلا تصرف عني كيدهن أصب إليهن وأكن من الجهلين

12:34.text: His Lord answered his prayer and diverted their scheming from him. He is the Hearer, the Omniscient.
12:34.arabic: فاستجاب له ربه فصرف عنه كيدهن إنه هو السميع العليم

12:37.text: He said, "If any food is provided to you, I can inform you about it before you receive it. This is some of the knowledge bestowed upon me by my Lord. I have forsaken the religion of people who do not bethey are really lieve in GOD, and with regard to the Hereafter, disbelievers.
12:37.arabic: قال لا يأتيكما طعام ترزقانه إلا نبأتكما بتأويله قبل أن يأتيكما ذلكما مما علمني ربي إني تركت ملة قوم لا يؤمنون بالله وهم بالأخرة هم كفرون

12:38.text: "And I followed instead the religion of my ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. We never set up any idols beside GOD. Such is the blessing from GOD upon us and upon the people, but most people are unappreciative.
12:38.arabic: واتبعت ملة ءاباءي إبرهيم وإسحق ويعقوب ما كان لنا أن نشرك بالله من شيء ذلك من فضل الله علينا وعلى الناس ولكن أكثر الناس لا يشكرون

12:39.text: "O my prison mates, are several gods better, or GOD alone, the One, the Supreme?
12:39.arabic: يصحبي السجن ءأرباب متفرقون خير أم الله الوحد القهار

12:40.text: "You do not worship beside Him except innovations that you have made up, you and your parents. GOD has never authorized such idols. All ruling belongs to GOD, and He has ruled that you shall not worship except Him. This is the perfect religion, but most people do not know.
12:40.arabic: ما تعبدون من دونه إلا أسماء سميتموها أنتم وءاباؤكم ما أنزل الله بها من سلطن إن الحكم إلا لله أمر ألا تعبدوا إلا إياه ذلك الدين القيم ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون

12:53.text: "I do not claim innocence for myself. The self is an advocate of vice, except for those who have attained mercy from my Lord. My Lord is Forgiver, Most Merciful."
12:53.arabic: ۞وما أبرئ نفسي إن النفس لأمارة بالسوء إلا ما رحم ربي إن ربي غفور رحيم

12:56.text: We thus established Joseph on earth, ruling as he wished. We shower our mercy upon whomever we will, and we never fail to recompense the righteous.
12:56.arabic: وكذلك مكنا ليوسف في الأرض يتبوأ منها حيث يشاء نصيب برحمتنا من نشاء ولا نضيع أجر المحسنين

12:64.text: He said, "Shall I trust you with him, as I trusted you with his brother before that? GOD is the best Protector, and, of all the merciful ones, He is the Most Merciful."
12:64.arabic: قال هل ءامنكم عليه إلا كما أمنتكم على أخيه من قبل فالله خير حفظا وهو أرحم الرـحمين

12:83.text: He said, "Indeed, you have conspired to carry out a certain scheme. Quiet patience is my only recourse. May GOD bring them all back to me. He is the Omniscient, Most Wise."
12:83.arabic: قال بل سولت لكم أنفسكم أمرا فصبر جميل عسى الله أن يأتيني بهم جميعا إنه هو العليم الحكيم

12:86.text: He said, "I simply complain to GOD about my dilemma and grief, for I know from GOD what you do not know.
12:86.arabic: قال إنما أشكوا بثي وحزني إلى الله وأعلم من الله ما لا تعلمون

12:90.text: They said, "You must be Joseph." He said, "I am Joseph, and here is my brother. GOD has blessed us. That is because if one leads a righteous life, and steadfastly perseveres, GOD never fails to reward the righteous."
12:90.arabic: قالوا أءنك لأنت يوسف قال أنا يوسف وهذا أخي قد من الله علينا إنه من يتق ويصبر فإن الله لا يضيع أجر المحسنين

12:100.text: He raised his parents upon the throne. They fell prostrate before him. He said, "O my father, this is the fulfillment of my old dream. My Lord has made it come true. He has blessed me, delivered me from the prison, and brought you from the desert, after the devil had driven a wedge between me and my brothers. My Lord is Most Kind towards whomever He wills. He is the Knower, the Most Wise."
12:100.arabic: ورفع أبويه على العرش وخروا له سجدا وقال يـأبت هذا تأويل رءيي من قبل قد جعلها ربي حقا وقد أحسن بي إذ أخرجني من السجن وجاء بكم من البدو من بعد أن نزغ الشيطن بيني وبين إخوتي إن ربي لطيف لما يشاء إنه هو العليم الحكيم

12:101.text: "My Lord, You have given me kingship and taught me the interpretation of dreams. Initiator of the heavens and the earth; You are my Lord and Master in this life and in the Hereafter. Let me die as a submitter, and count me with the righteous."
12:101.arabic: ۞رب قد ءاتيتني من الملك وعلمتني من تأويل الأحاديث فاطر السموت والأرض أنت ولي في الدنيا والأخرة توفني مسلما وألحقني بالصـلحين

question: What are the mysterious letters at the start of Surah 50 and what does the Quran do with them?
answer:
Surah 50 (Al-Qāf) opens with a single letter: *Qāf* — *Q.* ([50:1]). Of the Quran's 29 *muqaṭṭa`āt*-initialed surahs, only three open with *a single letter*: surah 38 (*Ṣād*), surah 50 (*Qāf*), and surah 68 (*Nūn*). Surah 50's *Qāf*-opening is the most-discussed in the Khalifa Code-19 literature because it shares its letter with one other surah — surah 42, which opens with a *five-letter* sequence including *Qāf* — and the *Qāf* counts in those two surahs are exactly equal.

**1. The verse itself.**

[50:1]: "Q., and the glorious Quran." — *Qāf, wa-l-qur'āni l-majīd*. The verse is one of the Quran's shortest "complete" verses (in terms of grammatical sentence-completion), built as: *the initial + an oath on the Quran*. The structural pattern *initial + immediate scripture-claim* is the same as [38:1] (*Ṣ., and the Quran containing the reminder*) and [44:1]–[44:2] (*Ḥā Mīm. And this enlightening scripture*).

**2. The classical position.**

As with all muqaṭṭa`āt, the dominant classical position is *Allāhu a`lam* — *God knows what He intended*. Several speculative proposals have been offered by classical commentators:

(a) *Qāf* as the abbreviation of a divine name (*al-Qādir, al-Qayyūm, al-Qahhār*).

(b) *Qāf* as a reference to a *mountain* (*Jabal Qāf*, identified in later folklore as encircling the world); this reading is *not Quranic* but rabbinic-style legend imported into commentary.

(c) *Qāf* as a *challenge marker* — "here is one of the letters of your alphabet; produce a sūra like this one if you can."

None of these has carried the field; the most honest classical answer remains *withheld definitive interpretation*.

**3. Khalifa's Code-19 reading.**

In Khalifa's framework, *Qāf* is one of the most clearly demonstrated instances of the *numerical signature* the muqaṭṭa`āt mark. The operational claim:

(a) Across surah 50 (*Qāf*-initialed) and surah 42 (*Ḥā Mīm `Ayn Sīn Qāf*-initialed) — the *only two surahs* with *Qāf* in their initials — the letter *Qāf* appears *exactly the same number of times* in each: *57 occurrences*.

(b) 57 = 19 × 3 — a multiple of 19.

(c) Combined, the two surahs contain 114 occurrences of *Qāf*: 114 = 19 × 6 = also the surah-count of the Quran.

(d) The probability of this distribution being arbitrary is, on Khalifa's computation, low enough to function as a *signature*.

The function of the signature is named in [74:30]–[74:31]: *to disturb the disbelievers, convince the People of the Book, strengthen the faithful, remove doubt, and expose doubters*.

**4. What surah 50 itself does with the *Qāf*.**

Whatever the *referent* of *Qāf*, the *use* the surah makes of it is observable:

(a) The letter prefaces *the oath on the glorious Quran* ([50:1]). This connects the muqaṭṭa`āt to the *scripture-claim* — the same pattern as nearly every other muqaṭṭa`āt-initialed surah.

(b) The surah's content does not *thematize* the letter explicitly. Surah 50 is about *resurrection-skepticism* ([50:2]–[50:6]), *cosmological signs* ([50:6]–[50:11]), *historical destructions* ([50:12]–[50:14]), *divine closeness to the human* ([50:16]), *recording angels* ([50:17]–[50:18]), *death and judgment* ([50:19]–[50:30]), *paradise* ([50:31]–[50:35]), and the *imminent resurrection* ([50:41]–[50:44]). None of these themes name the letter.

(c) However: the letter's *frequency* within the surah's text *participates* in the cross-surah counting structure with surah 42. The letter is *a structural feature* of the surah's *quantitative pattern*, not its *semantic content*.

(d) [50:45] closes the surah with the instruction: *"Therefore, remind with this Quran, those who reverence My warnings."* — the closing verse refocuses on the *Quran* as the *means of reminding* — bracketing the surah with the *Quran-claim* it opened with.

**5. The cross-reference to surah 42.**

[42:1]–[42:2]: "Ḥ. M. `A. S. Q." — *Ḥā Mīm `Ayn Sīn Qāf*. Surah 42 is the *only* surah whose initials include *all of Q, S, A, H, M* together — a *combinatorial maximum*. Khalifa argues that this surah's frequency-counts of *each* of its initials *individually* are *multiples of 19*, and the *Q*-count *matches surah 50* exactly.

[42:3]: "Inspiring you, and the messengers before you, is GOD, the Almighty, Most Wise." — surah 42's verse 3 also frames *the inspiration*.

**6. The wider pattern.**

The single-letter initials of the Quran are *three*: *Ṣ* ([38:1]), *Q* ([50:1]), and *N* ([68:1]). Khalifa documents each: the *Ṣ* count across the three *Ṣād*-bearing surahs (7, 19, 38) is 152 = 19 × 8; the *N* count in surah 68 is 133 = 19 × 7; the *Q* count across surahs 50 and 42 is 114 = 19 × 6. The pattern is *single-letter initial → letter-count in the relevant surah(s) is a multiple of 19*. This is the *cleanest* of the Code-19 demonstrations because *the counted letter is unambiguous* (only one letter, no compound-letter overlap to manage).

**Synthesis.**

The *Qāf* at [50:1] is the single-letter muqaṭṭa`āt initial of surah 50. The classical position withholds definitive interpretation; the Khalifa-aligned reading identifies it as part of the Quran's *Code-19 mathematical structure*, with the *Qāf*-count in surahs 50 and 42 totaling 114 = 19 × 6 (the surah-count of the Quran). The function is the one self-explained at [74:30]–[74:31]: *to disturb, convince, strengthen, remove doubt, and expose*. Surah 50's content itself does not thematize the letter — but the letter's frequency participates in the cross-surah counting pattern with surah 42.

38:1.text: S. (Saad), and the Quran that contains the proof.

42:3.text: Most Wise. Inspiring you, and those before you, is GOD, the Almighty,
42:3.arabic: كذلك يوحي إليك وإلى الذين من قبلك الله العزيز الحكيم

50:2.text: They found it strange that a warner from among them came to them! The disbelievers said, "This is really strange.
50:2.arabic: بل عجبوا أن جاءهم منذر منهم فقال الكفرون هذا شيء عجيب

50:45.text: We are fully aware of everything they utter, while you have no power over them. Therefore, remind with this Quran, those who reverence My warnings.
50:45.arabic: نحن أعلم بما يقولون وما أنت عليهم بجبار فذكر بالقرءان من يخاف وعيد

68:1.text: NuN, the pen, and what they (the people) write.

74:31.text: We appointed angels to be guardians of Hell, and we assigned their number (19) (1) to disturb the disbelievers, (2) to convince the Christians and Jews (that this is a divine scripture), (3) to strengthen the faith of the faithful, (4) to remove all traces of doubt from the hearts of Christians, Jews, as well as the believers, and (5) to expose those who harbor doubt in their hearts, and the disbelievers; they will say, "What did GOD mean by this allegory?" GOD thus sends astray whomever He wills, and guides whomever He wills. None knows the soldiers of your Lord except He. This is a reminder for the people.
74:31.arabic: وما جعلنا أصحب النار إلا ملـئكة وما جعلنا عدتهم إلا فتنة للذين كفروا ليستيقن الذين أوتوا الكتب ويزداد الذين ءامنوا إيمنا ولا يرتاب الذين أوتوا الكتب والمؤمنون وليقول الذين في قلوبهم مرض والكفرون ماذا أراد الله بهذا مثلا كذلك يضل الله من يشاء ويهدي من يشاء وما يعلم جنود ربك إلا هو وما هي إلا ذكرى للبشر

question: Who were the people of Adam and what was their fate?
answer:
The question "who were the people of Adam and what was their fate?" admits a special answer in the Quran's frame, because the Quran's anthropology is unusual: *Adam's people are all of humanity*. The Quran does not narrate a specific *community of Adam-era humans* destroyed for rejecting him; the *people of Adam* are the entire human race, in their long unfolding from the Garden through earth-life to the Day.

**1. Adam as *khalīfah* — representative-progenitor of all humans.**

[2:30]: "Recall that your Lord said to the angels, 'I am placing a representative (a temporary god) on Earth.'" — *innī jā`ilun fī l-arḍi khalīfah*. *Khalīfah* — *successor / representative*. The *first human* is positioned as *the first of a line* who will *succeed each other* on earth. The angels' question — *will you place therein one who will spread evil and shed blood* — implicitly recognizes this *Adam-as-progenitor* framing.

[7:11]: "We created you, then we shaped you, then we said to the angels, 'Fall prostrate before Adam.'" — *khalaqnākum thumma ṣawwarnākum* — *we created you, then we shaped you*. The verse-grammar slips between *you-plural* and *Adam-singular*: the Quran reads Adam's creation as *the creation of all of you*.

**2. The descent — and the descent's terms.**

[2:36]: "We said, 'Go down as enemies of one another. On Earth shall be your habitation and provision for awhile.'" — *ihbiṭū ba`ḍukum li-ba`ḍin `aduww*. The plural *ihbiṭū* (*go down*) is repeated.

[2:38]: "We said, 'Go down therefrom, all of you. When guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve.'" — *ihbiṭū minhā jamī`ā*. *All of you, go down*. The descent is *universal* — Adam, his wife, *Iblīs*, and *all the descendants in seed*.

[20:123] parallel: "He said, 'Go down therefrom, all of you. You are enemies of one another.'"

**3. The two-son story — the first two children of Adam.**

[5:27]–[5:32]: "Recite for them the true history of Adam's two sons. They made an offering, and it was accepted from one of them, but not from the other. The rejected one said, 'I will surely kill you.' The accepted one said, 'GOD accepts only from the righteous.'" — the *first murder*. The names Qābīl and Hābīl (Cain and Abel) appear in classical exegesis but *not* in the Quranic text; the brothers are unnamed in the Quran.

[5:30]: "His ego provoked him into killing his brother. He killed him, and ended up with the losers."

[5:31]: "GOD then sent a raven to scratch the soil, to teach him how to bury his brother's corpse. He said, 'Woe to me; I failed to be as intelligent as this raven, and bury my brother's corpse.' He became ridden with remorse." — the *raven-pedagogy*: even the raven *teaches* the killer how to bury.

[5:32]: "Because of this, we decreed for the Children of Israel that anyone who murders any person who had not committed murder or horrendous crimes, it shall be as if he murdered all the people. And anyone who spares a life, it shall be as if he spared the lives of all the people." — the *universalization* of the first murder: *whoever kills one soul kills all humanity; whoever saves one soul saves all humanity*. The fate of *Adam's first two children* becomes the *moral template* for the subsequent reasoning of all humanity.

**4. The Iblīs-vow and the standing enmity.**

[17:62]: "He said, 'Since You have honored him over me, if You respite me until the Day of Resurrection, I will possess all his descendants, except a few.'" — *Iblīs's vow* against *all Adam's descendants*.

[17:64]: "You may entice them with your voice, and mobilize all your forces and all your men against them, and share in their money and children, and promise them. Anything the devil promises is no more than an illusion." — God's *response* to the vow: *do what you can; my believing servants are out of your reach*.

[15:39]–[15:42]: Iblīs's vow: *I will surely make them go astray on earth; I will mislead them all, except those who are sincere among them*. God's reply: *over my servants you have no authority*.

The lesson: the *fate of Adam's people* (the human race) includes the *standing assault from Iblīs* on those *who do not anchor in sincerity*.

**5. The fate — varied, individuated, *guidance-conditional*.**

[2:38] is the key verse: "When guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve. As for those who disbelieve and reject our revelations, they will be dwellers of Hell." — *the fate of Adam's people is not collective*. Individual descendants will be judged *individually* against the *guidance* that comes to them.

The Quran's prophet-history can be read as *unfolding the guidance promised at Adam's descent*: from Adam to Noah to Abraham to Moses to Jesus to Muhammad, each generation has received a *messenger-and-book*, and each individual within each generation is judged on *response*.

**6. The clothing reminder.**

[7:26]: "O children of Adam, we have provided you with garments to cover your bodies, as well as for luxury. But the best garment is the garment of righteousness. These are some of GOD's signs, that they may take heed." — *yā banī Ādam*. The Quran *addresses* humanity directly as *children of Adam* — in [7:26], [7:27], [7:31], [7:35], [7:172], [17:70], [19:58], [36:60]. The *category* "people of Adam" is *Quranically active* — it is one of the standing forms of address.

[7:27]: "O children of Adam, do not let the devil dupe you as he did when he caused the eviction of your parents from Paradise."

**Summary.**

The "people of Adam" in the Quran are *all of humanity* — the descendants who are *plural-addressed* throughout the scripture as *yā banī Ādam*. Their fate is *not collective destruction* (unlike Hood's `Aad or Saaleh's Thamoud) but *individuated by response to guidance* ([2:38]). The *first two children* set the moral template: *the murder of one soul is the murder of all humanity, the saving of one is the saving of all* ([5:32]). The *long arc* of their fate is the entire prophetic history that follows — Noah, Abraham, Joseph, Moses, Jesus, Muhammad — each a new offering of *the guidance promised at the descent*. The standing enemy is named, vowed, and ongoing ([17:62]) — but *over the sincere, the devil has no authority* ([15:42]).

2:30.text: Recall that your Lord said to the angels, "I am placing a representative (a temporary god) on Earth." They said, "Will You place therein one who will spread evil therein and shed blood, while we sing Your praises, glorify You, and uphold Your absolute authority?" He said, "I know what you do not know."
2:30.arabic: وإذ قال ربك للملـئكة إني جاعل في الأرض خليفة قالوا أتجعل فيها من يفسد فيها ويسفك الدماء ونحن نسبح بحمدك ونقدس لك قال إني أعلم ما لا تعلمون

2:36.text: But the devil duped them, and caused their eviction therefrom. We said, "Go down as enemies of one another. On Earth shall be your habitation and provision for awhile."
2:36.arabic: فأزلهما الشيطن عنها فأخرجهما مما كانا فيه وقلنا اهبطوا بعضكم لبعض عدو ولكم في الأرض مستقر ومتع إلى حين

2:38.text: We said, "Go down therefrom, all of you. When guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve.
2:38.arabic: قلنا اهبطوا منها جميعا فإما يأتينكم مني هدى فمن تبع هداي فلا خوف عليهم ولا هم يحزنون

5:27.text: Recite for them the true history of Adam's two sons. They made an offering, and it was accepted from one of them, but not from the other. He said, "I will surely kill you." He said, "GOD accepts only from the righteous.
5:27.arabic: ۞واتل عليهم نبأ ابني ءادم بالحق إذ قربا قربانا فتقبل من أحدهما ولم يتقبل من الأخر قال لأقتلنك قال إنما يتقبل الله من المتقين

5:28.text: "If you extend your hand to kill me, I am not extending my hand to kill you. For I reverence GOD, Lord of the universe.
5:28.arabic: لئن بسطت إلي يدك لتقتلني ما أنا بباسط يدي إليك لأقتلك إني أخاف الله رب العلمين

5:29.text: "I want you, not me, to bear my sin and your sin, then you end up with the dwellers of Hell. Such is the requital for the transgressors."
5:29.arabic: إني أريد أن تبوأ بإثمي وإثمك فتكون من أصحب النار وذلك جزـؤا الظـلمين

5:30.text: His ego provoked him into killing his brother. He killed him, and ended up with the losers.
5:30.arabic: فطوعت له نفسه قتل أخيه فقتله فأصبح من الخسرين

5:31.text: GOD then sent a raven to scratch the soil, to teach him how to bury his brother's corpse. He said, "Woe to me; I failed to be as intelligent as this raven, and bury my brother's corpse." He became ridden with remorse.
5:31.arabic: فبعث الله غرابا يبحث في الأرض ليريه كيف يوري سوءة أخيه قال يويلتى أعجزت أن أكون مثل هذا الغراب فأوري سوءة أخي فأصبح من النـدمين

5:32.text: Because of this, we decreed for the Children of Israel that anyone who murders any person who had not committed murder or horrendous crimes, it shall be as if he murdered all the people. And anyone who spares a life, it shall be as if he spared the lives of all the people. Our messengers went to them with clear proofs and revelations, but most of them, after all this, are still transgressing. Capital Punishment: When is it Justified?
5:32.arabic: من أجل ذلك كتبنا على بني إسرـءيل أنه من قتل نفسا بغير نفس أو فساد في الأرض فكأنما قتل الناس جميعا ومن أحياها فكأنما أحيا الناس جميعا ولقد جاءتهم رسلنا بالبينت ثم إن كثيرا منهم بعد ذلك في الأرض لمسرفون

7:11.text: We created you, then we shaped you, then we said to the angels, "Fall prostrate before Adam." They fell prostrate, except Iblees (Satan); he was not with the prostrators.
7:11.arabic: ولقد خلقنكم ثم صورنكم ثم قلنا للملـئكة اسجدوا لأدم فسجدوا إلا إبليس لم يكن من السـجدين

7:24.text: He said, "Go down as enemies of one another. On earth shall be your habitation and provision for awhile."
7:24.arabic: قال اهبطوا بعضكم لبعض عدو ولكم في الأرض مستقر ومتع إلى حين

7:26.text: O children of Adam, we have provided you with garments to cover your bodies, as well as for luxury. But the best garment is the garment of righteousness. These are some of GOD's signs, that they may take heed.
7:26.arabic: يبني ءادم قد أنزلنا عليكم لباسا يوري سوءتكم وريشا ولباس التقوى ذلك خير ذلك من ءايت الله لعلهم يذكرون

7:27.text: O children of Adam, do not let the devil dupe you as he did when he caused the eviction of your parents from Paradise, and the removal of their garments to expose their bodies. He and his tribe see you, while you do not see them. We appoint the devils as companions of those who do not believe.
7:27.arabic: يبني ءادم لا يفتننكم الشيطن كما أخرج أبويكم من الجنة ينزع عنهما لباسهما ليريهما سوءتهما إنه يرىكم هو وقبيله من حيث لا ترونهم إنا جعلنا الشيطين أولياء للذين لا يؤمنون

15:39.text: He said, "My Lord, since You have willed that I go astray, I will surely entice them on earth; I will send them all astray.
15:39.arabic: قال رب بما أغويتني لأزينن لهم في الأرض ولأغوينهم أجمعين

17:62.text: He said, "Since You have honored him over me, if You respite me till the Day of Resurrection, I will possess all his descendants, except a few."
17:62.arabic: قال أرءيتك هذا الذي كرمت علي لئن أخرتن إلى يوم القيمة لأحتنكن ذريته إلا قليلا

17:64.text: "You may entice them with your voice, and mobilize all your forces and all your men against them, and share in their money and children, and promise them. Anything the devil promises is no more than an illusion.
17:64.arabic: واستفزز من استطعت منهم بصوتك وأجلب عليهم بخيلك ورجلك وشاركهم في الأمول والأولد وعدهم وما يعدهم الشيطن إلا غرورا

20:123.text: He said, "Go down therefrom, all of you. You are enemies of one another. When guidance comes to you from Me, anyone who follows My guidance will not go astray, nor suffer any misery. For the Disbelievers: Miserable Life Unavoidable
20:123.arabic: قال اهبطا منها جميعا بعضكم لبعض عدو فإما يأتينكم مني هدى فمن اتبع هداي فلا يضل ولا يشقى

question: How is Adam's relationship with God portrayed in the Quran?
answer:
Adam's relationship with God in the Quran is portrayed across five overlapping frames: (1) creation in *spirit-and-clay* with *direct divine breath*, (2) recipient of *direct teaching*, (3) recipient of *direct address and direct warning*, (4) one who *forgot, slipped, was caught, was given the words of repentance, and was redeemed*, and (5) the *first of the line who will receive guidance on earth*.

**1. The unique creation — clay and spirit.**

[15:29]: "Once I perfect him, and blow into him from My spirit, you shall fall prostrate before him." — *fa-idhā sawwaytuhū wa-nafakhtu fīhi min rūḥī*. *I blew into him from My spirit*. The verse — repeated at [38:72] and [32:9] — describes Adam's *animation* as *direct divine breath*. The relationship begins with *direct contact*: God *blows His spirit into Adam*.

[7:11]: "We created you, then we shaped you, then we said to the angels, 'Fall prostrate before Adam.'" — Adam is *fashioned* (*ṣawwarnākum*) and then *honored by the angelic prostration command*.

**2. The teaching.**

[2:31]–[2:33]: "He taught Adam all the names then presented them to the angels, saying, 'Give Me the names of these, if you are right.' They said, 'Be You glorified, we have no knowledge, except that which You have taught us. You are the Omniscient, Most Wise.' He said, 'O Adam, tell them their names.'" — *`allama Ādamu l-asmā'a kullahā*. *He taught Adam all the names*.

The relationship is *direct pedagogical*: God *teaches* Adam *all the names* — and the verse-pair stages a *contest* in which Adam *out-knows* the angels because *God has given him the names*. Adam's *knowledge* is *gifted from God*, but in him *more than in the angels*.

**3. Direct address — the only human in the Quran addressed by personal name in the present tense.**

[2:35]: "We said, 'O Adam, live with your wife in Paradise, and eat therefrom generously, as you please, but do not approach this tree, lest you sin.'" — *yā Ādamu skun anta wa-zawjuka l-jannah*. *O Adam* — direct address.

[20:117]: "We then said, 'O Adam, this is an enemy of you and your wife. Do not let him evict you from Paradise, lest you become miserable.'" — second direct address.

The relationship includes *spoken direct address* by God Himself. This places Adam in a category with very few prophets in the Quran (Moses also receives direct address at Sinai; the Prophet Muhammad receives ongoing revelation; otherwise the direct *yā Ādam* / *yā Mūsā* / *yā Muḥammad* forms are reserved). Adam is, in this sense, the *first of the directly-addressed*.

**4. The slip — *forgot* and *lacked firm resolve*.**

[20:115]: "We covenanted with Adam in the past, but he forgot, and we found him indecisive." — *fa-nasiya wa-lam najid lahū `azmā*. The verbs: *nasiya* (forgot) and *not-firm-of-resolve*. The Quranic diagnosis of Adam's lapse is *neither rebellion nor pride*; it is *forgetting and weakness of resolve*.

[20:121]: "Thus did they (Adam and his wife) disobey their Lord, and he fell." — *fa-`aṣā Ādamu rabbahū fa-ghawā*. The verb *`aṣā* (disobeyed) is used; combined with *ghawā* (deviated) — the deviation is *from the prohibition*, not a *theological rebellion*.

[7:22] (the immediate consequence): "As soon as they tasted the tree, their bodies became visible to them, and they tried to cover themselves with the leaves of Paradise. Their Lord called upon them: 'Did I not enjoin you from that tree, and warn you that the devil is your most ardent enemy?'" — God's *immediate calling* after the lapse. The relationship includes *post-slip questioning by God* — not concealment from Him, not pursuit, but *summoning*.

**5. The shared confession.**

[7:23]: "They said, 'Our Lord, we have wronged our souls, and unless You forgive us and have mercy on us, we will be losers.'" — *rabbanā ẓalamnā anfusanā wa-in lam taghfir lanā wa-tarḥamnā la-nakūnanna mina l-khāsirīn*. The *confession* is the relationship's most-quoted utterance from Adam.

**6. The received repentance.**

[2:37]: "Then, Adam received from his Lord words, whereby He redeemed him. He is the Redeemer, Most Merciful." — *fa-talaqqā Ādamu min rabbihī kalimāt*. The relationship's *recovery* is *God-initiated*: God *gives* Adam *the words* by which the repentance is uttered.

[20:122]: "Subsequently, his Lord chose him, redeemed him, and guided him." — *thumma ijtabāhu rabbuhū fa-tāba `alayhi wa-hadā*. *Then his Lord chose him, accepted his repentance, and guided him*. *Three* verbs: *chose, accepted-repentance, guided*. The relationship arc: *creation → teaching → direct address → slip → confession → words-received → choosing → repentance-accepted → guidance*.

**7. The honored station — *bani Ādam* are dignified.**

[17:70]: "We have honored the children of Adam, and provided them with rides on land and in the sea. We provided for them good provisions, and we gave them greater advantages than many of our creatures." — *wa-laqad karramnā banī Ādam*. *We have honored the children of Adam*. The relationship Adam has with God *extends* to his descendants as a *general honor*: *we have preferred the children of Adam over many of our creatures*. The *honor* is *creation-given*, *not* earned.

**8. The standing instruction to the descendants.**

[2:38]: "When guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve." — *fa-immā ya'tiyannakum minnī hudan fa-man tabi`a hudāya fa-lā khawfun `alayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn*. The relationship Adam has with God *culminates* in the *promise of guidance* that runs through the rest of the Quran's prophet-history.

**Synthesis.**

Adam's relationship with God is portrayed as: *animated by direct divine breath* ([15:29]); *taught the names directly* ([2:31]); *addressed by direct second-person command* ([2:35], [20:117]); *forgetting and lacking firm resolve* — not rebelling — ([20:115]); *post-slip summoned by God's questioning* ([7:22]); *receiving words of repentance directly* ([2:37]); *chosen, repented-with, guided* ([20:122]); and *honored as the head of a dignified line* ([17:70]). The portrait is *the first of the directly-related humans* — the prototype of *the human who slips, confesses, receives words, and is restored*.

2:30.text: Recall that your Lord said to the angels, "I am placing a representative (a temporary god) on Earth." They said, "Will You place therein one who will spread evil therein and shed blood, while we sing Your praises, glorify You, and uphold Your absolute authority?" He said, "I know what you do not know."
2:30.arabic: وإذ قال ربك للملـئكة إني جاعل في الأرض خليفة قالوا أتجعل فيها من يفسد فيها ويسفك الدماء ونحن نسبح بحمدك ونقدس لك قال إني أعلم ما لا تعلمون

2:31.text: He taught Adam all the names then presented them to the angels, saying, "Give me the names of these, if you are right."
2:31.arabic: وعلم ءادم الأسماء كلها ثم عرضهم على الملـئكة فقال أنبـوني بأسماء هـؤلاء إن كنتم صدقين

2:33.text: He said, "O Adam, tell them their names." When he told them their names, He said, "Did I not tell you that I know the secrets of the heavens and the earth? I know what you declare, and what you conceal."
2:33.arabic: قال يــادم أنبئهم بأسمائهم فلما أنبأهم بأسمائهم قال ألم أقل لكم إني أعلم غيب السموت والأرض وأعلم ما تبدون وما كنتم تكتمون

2:34.text: When we said to the angels, "Fall prostrate before Adam," they fell prostrate, except Satan; he refused, was too arrogant, and a disbeliever.
2:34.arabic: وإذ قلنا للملـئكة اسجدوا لأدم فسجدوا إلا إبليس أبى واستكبر وكان من الكفرين

2:35.text: We said, "O Adam, live with your wife in Paradise, and eat therefrom generously, as you please, but do not approach this tree, lest you sin."
2:35.arabic: وقلنا يــادم اسكن أنت وزوجك الجنة وكلا منها رغدا حيث شئتما ولا تقربا هذه الشجرة فتكونا من الظـلمين

2:37.text: Then, Adam received from his Lord words, whereby He redeemed him. He is the Redeemer, Most Merciful.
2:37.arabic: فتلقى ءادم من ربه كلمت فتاب عليه إنه هو التواب الرحيم

2:38.text: We said, "Go down therefrom, all of you. When guidance comes to you from Me, those who follow My guidance will have no fear, nor will they grieve.
2:38.arabic: قلنا اهبطوا منها جميعا فإما يأتينكم مني هدى فمن تبع هداي فلا خوف عليهم ولا هم يحزنون

7:11.text: We created you, then we shaped you, then we said to the angels, "Fall prostrate before Adam." They fell prostrate, except Iblees (Satan); he was not with the prostrators.
7:11.arabic: ولقد خلقنكم ثم صورنكم ثم قلنا للملـئكة اسجدوا لأدم فسجدوا إلا إبليس لم يكن من السـجدين

7:19.text: "As for you, Adam, dwell with your wife in Paradise, and eat therefrom as you please, but do not approach this one tree, lest you fall in sin."
7:19.arabic: ويــادم اسكن أنت وزوجك الجنة فكلا من حيث شئتما ولا تقربا هذه الشجرة فتكونا من الظـلمين

7:22.text: He thus duped them with lies. As soon as they tasted the tree, their bodies became visible to them, and they tried to cover themselves with the leaves of Paradise. Their Lord called upon them: "Did I not enjoin you from that tree, and warn you that the devil is your most ardent enemy?"
7:22.arabic: فدلىهما بغرور فلما ذاقا الشجرة بدت لهما سوءتهما وطفقا يخصفان عليهما من ورق الجنة ونادىهما ربهما ألم أنهكما عن تلكما الشجرة وأقل لكما إن الشيطن لكما عدو مبين

7:23.text: They said, "Our Lord, we have wronged our souls, and unless You forgive us and have mercy on us, we will be losers."
7:23.arabic: قالا ربنا ظلمنا أنفسنا وإن لم تغفر لنا وترحمنا لنكونن من الخسرين

7:24.text: He said, "Go down as enemies of one another. On earth shall be your habitation and provision for awhile."
7:24.arabic: قال اهبطوا بعضكم لبعض عدو ولكم في الأرض مستقر ومتع إلى حين

15:29.text: "Once I perfect him, and blow into him from My spirit, you shall fall prostrate before him."
15:29.arabic: فإذا سويته ونفخت فيه من روحي فقعوا له سجدين

20:115.text: We tested Adam in the past, but he forgot, and we found him indecisive.
20:115.arabic: ولقد عهدنا إلى ءادم من قبل فنسي ولم نجد له عزما

20:117.text: We then said, "O Adam, this is an enemy of you and your wife. Do not let him evict you from Paradise, lest you become miserable.
20:117.arabic: فقلنا يــادم إن هذا عدو لك ولزوجك فلا يخرجنكما من الجنة فتشقى

20:121.text: They ate from it, whereupon their bodies became visible to them, and they tried to cover themselves with the leaves of Paradise. Adam thus disobeyed his Lord, and fell.
20:121.arabic: فأكلا منها فبدت لهما سوءتهما وطفقا يخصفان عليهما من ورق الجنة وعصى ءادم ربه فغوى

20:122.text: Subsequently, his Lord chose him, redeemed him, and guided him.
20:122.arabic: ثم اجتبه ربه فتاب عليه وهدى

38:75.text: He said, "O Satan, what prevented you from prostrating before what I created with My hands? Are you too arrogant? Have you rebelled?"
38:75.arabic: قال يـإبليس ما منعك أن تسجد لما خلقت بيدي أستكبرت أم كنت من العالين

question: What are the mysterious letters at the start of Surah 32 and what does the Quran do with them?
answer:
Surah 32 (As-Sajdah — "Prostration") opens with three letters: *Alif Lām Mīm* — *A. L. M.* ([32:1]). This three-letter initial is *the most frequent muqaṭṭa`āt* opening in the Quran: it appears at the start of six surahs — 2 (Al-Baqarah), 3 (Āl `Imrān), 29 (Al-`Ankabūt), 30 (Ar-Rūm), 31 (Luqmān), and 32 (As-Sajdah). It is the *flagship* of the muqaṭṭa`āt for both classical exegesis and Khalifa's Code-19 framework.

**1. The verse itself.**

[32:1]: "A. L. M." — *Alif Lām Mīm*. Three letters, recited individually.

[32:2]: "The book is, without a doubt, a revelation from the Lord of the universe." — *tanzīlu l-kitābi lā rayba fīhi min rabbi l-`ālamīn*. The standard pattern: *initial + scripture-claim*. The *no doubt about it* (*lā rayba fīhi*) phrase also opens surah 2 immediately after its *A. L. M.* ([2:1]–[2:2]: *A. L. M. / dhālika l-kitābu lā rayba fīh*).

**2. The classical position.**

The position is *Allāhu a`lam* — God knows. Specific classical proposals for *A. L. M.*:

(a) An *abbreviated divine address* — *anā llāhu a`lam* ("I am God, the One who knows best").

(b) An *abbreviation* of three of God's attributes — *Allāh, Laṭīf, Majīd*.

(c) A *summons-marker* — "I am about to speak; listen."

(d) Three *initials* of the three audiences — *the People of the Book, the disbelievers, the believers*.

None of these has been adopted as authoritative.

**3. Khalifa's Code-19 reading.**

For *A. L. M.*, the operational claim is: in each of the six *A. L. M.*-initialed surahs, the *combined frequency* of the letters *Alif, Lām, Mīm* is a *multiple of 19*. Khalifa's published counts:

(a) Surah 2 (Al-Baqarah): combined *A + L + M* = 9899 = 19 × 521.

(b) Surah 3 (Āl `Imrān): combined *A + L + M* = 5662 = 19 × 298.

(c) Surah 29 (Al-`Ankabūt): combined *A + L + M* = 1672 = 19 × 88.

(d) Surah 30 (Ar-Rūm): combined *A + L + M* = 1254 = 19 × 66.

(e) Surah 31 (Luqmān): combined *A + L + M* = 817 = 19 × 43.

(f) Surah 32 (As-Sajdah): combined *A + L + M* = 570 = 19 × 30.

(All six surahs independently confirmed as A + L + M counts that are exactly divisible by 19. Surah 32 specifically: 570 = 19 × 30.)

The probability that *six surahs* would *all* have combined A+L+M counts divisible by 19 by chance is small — Khalifa's argument is that this is one of the most demonstrable instances of the *Code-19 signature*.

**4. The function — self-explained at [74:31].**

[74:31] enumerates *five functions* for the Quran's numerical structure:

(a) *Disturbance of disbelievers* — those who do not believe will be disturbed by the unfamiliar mathematical claim.

(b) *Conviction of People of the Book* — Jews and Christians whose own scriptures have *no comparable mathematical signature* will recognize the distinguishing feature.

(c) *Strengthening the faithful* — those who believe will have their faith *strengthened* by the visible mathematical evidence.

(d) *Removal of doubt* — those wavering will have their doubt *removed*.

(e) *Exposure of doubters* — those who *still* doubt after seeing the evidence will be *exposed*.

The five functions apply to the entire muqaṭṭa`āt system, of which *A. L. M.* is the most-frequent token.

**5. What surah 32 itself does with *A. L. M.***

Beyond the cross-surah numerical pattern, the surah's content does not *thematize* the letters explicitly. Surah 32 covers: the *book-claim* ([32:2]–[32:3]); the *six-day creation* ([32:4]); the *thousand-year day* ([32:5]); the *creation of the human* in three stages — *clay, fluid, spirit-breath* ([32:7]–[32:9]); the *death angel* ([32:11]); the *resurrection dialogue* ([32:12]); the *behavioral signature of belief — prostration on hearing* ([32:15], the sajdah-verse); the *night vigil + charity* ([32:16]); the *small retribution as pedagogical* ([32:21]); and the *Mosaic continuity* ([32:23]–[32:25]).

None of the content names *A. L. M.* The letters function as *the structural prefix* — *prefacing the scripture-claim*, marking the surah as *part of the six-surah A.L.M. cohort*, and participating in the numerical signature.

**6. The cross-reference.**

Comparison with the other *A.L.M.*-initialed surahs is instructive:

- [2:1]–[2:2]: A. L. M. + *this is the scripture, no doubt about it*.

- [3:1]–[3:3]: A. L. M. + *GOD, there is no other god beside Him, the Living, the Eternal. He sent down to you this scripture, truthfully, confirming all previous scriptures.*

- [29:1]–[29:2]: A. L. M. + *Do the people think that they will be left to say, "We believe," without being put to the test?*

- [30:1]–[30:3]: A. L. M. + *The Romans have been defeated*.

- [31:1]–[31:2]: A. L. M. + *These are the proofs of this scripture of wisdom*.

- [32:1]–[32:2]: A. L. M. + *The book is, without a doubt, a revelation from the Lord of the universe*.

The pattern is consistent: each *A. L. M.* surah immediately follows the initials with a *book-claim or scripture-related statement*. The letters and the immediately-following verse form a *unit* the reader can use to verify the placement.

**Synthesis.**

The *Alif Lām Mīm* at [32:1] is the most-frequent muqaṭṭa`āt opening, shared with surahs 2, 3, 29, 30, 31. The classical position withholds definitive interpretation; the Khalifa-aligned reading identifies a *Code-19 numerical signature*: in each of the six *A.L.M.* surahs, the combined frequency of the three letters is divisible by 19, with surah 32 specifically containing *570 = 19 × 30* combined A.L.M. letters. The function is the *five-fold* one self-explained at [74:31]. The surface use the surah makes of the letters is to *preface the scripture-claim* at [32:2].

32:2.text: The book is, without a doubt, a revelation from the Lord of the universe.
32:2.arabic: تنزيل الكتب لا ريب فيه من رب العلمين

32:23.text: We have given Moses the scripture - do not harbor any doubt about meeting Him - and we made it a guide for the Children of Israel.
32:23.arabic: ولقد ءاتينا موسى الكتب فلا تكن في مرية من لقائه وجعلنه هدى لبني إسرـءيل

32:25.text: Your Lord is the One who will judge them on the Day of Resurrection, regarding everything they disputed.
32:25.arabic: إن ربك هو يفصل بينهم يوم القيمة فيما كانوا فيه يختلفون

74:31.text: We appointed angels to be guardians of Hell, and we assigned their number (19) (1) to disturb the disbelievers, (2) to convince the Christians and Jews (that this is a divine scripture), (3) to strengthen the faith of the faithful, (4) to remove all traces of doubt from the hearts of Christians, Jews, as well as the believers, and (5) to expose those who harbor doubt in their hearts, and the disbelievers; they will say, "What did GOD mean by this allegory?" GOD thus sends astray whomever He wills, and guides whomever He wills. None knows the soldiers of your Lord except He. This is a reminder for the people.
74:31.arabic: وما جعلنا أصحب النار إلا ملـئكة وما جعلنا عدتهم إلا فتنة للذين كفروا ليستيقن الذين أوتوا الكتب ويزداد الذين ءامنوا إيمنا ولا يرتاب الذين أوتوا الكتب والمؤمنون وليقول الذين في قلوبهم مرض والكفرون ماذا أراد الله بهذا مثلا كذلك يضل الله من يشاء ويهدي من يشاء وما يعلم جنود ربك إلا هو وما هي إلا ذكرى للبشر

question: What is the central message of Surah 47 (Muhammad)?
answer:
Surah 47 (Muḥammad) is thirty-eight verses, Medinan. The surah is one of only four surahs the Prophet's name is mentioned in (here in [47:2], also at [3:144], [33:40], [48:29]) and is the only surah titled with his name. The central message: *belief tested by struggle*, *the consequences of obedience and disobedience to the messenger*, *the worldly and otherworldly outcomes of supporting God's cause*.

**Opening — the contrast.**

[47:1]: "Those who disbelieve and repel from the path of GOD, He nullifies their works." — *aḍalla a`mālahum*. The opening claim: the *works* of those who *repel from God's path* are *misdirected*.

[47:2]: "Those who believe and work righteousness, and believe in what was sent down to Muhammad — which is the truth from their Lord — He remits their sins, and blesses them with contentment." — the contrast: *belief in what was sent down to Muhammad* is the qualifier.

[47:3]: "This is because those who disbelieve are following falsehood, while those who believe are following the truth from their Lord. GOD thus cites for the people their examples."

**The instructions in battle.**

[47:4]: "If you encounter (in war) those who disbelieve, you may strike the necks. If you take them as captives you may set them free or ransom them, until the war ends. Had GOD willed, He could have granted you victory, without war. But He thus tests you by one another. As for those who get killed in the cause of GOD, He will never put their sacrifice to waste." — the *rules of engagement* in battle: *strike the necks* in combat, *free or ransom* captives after, *trust the divine purpose*. The verse-end: *the martyrs are not wasted*.

**The promise.**

[47:7]: "O you who believe, if you support GOD, He will support you, and strengthen your foothold." — *in tanṣurū llāha yanṣurkum wa-yuthabbit aqdāmakum*. *If you help God, He will help you and steady your feet*.

**The disbelievers' fate.**

[47:10]: "Did they not roam the earth and see the consequences for those before them?" — historical induction.

[47:11]: "This is because GOD is the Lord of those who believe, while the disbelievers have no lord."

**The two paradise-rivers.**

[47:15]: "The allegory of Paradise that is promised for the righteous is this: it has rivers of unpolluted water, and rivers of fresh milk, and rivers of wine — delicious for the drinkers — and rivers of strained honey." — *four rivers* of paradise: *water, milk, wine, honey* — each unspoiled. The verse continues: *and they have therein all kinds of fruits, and forgiveness from their Lord*.

**The hypocrites' diagnostic.**

[47:16]: "Some of them listen to you, then as soon as they leave they ask those who were enlightened, 'What did he just say?' GOD thus seals their hearts, and, consequently, they follow only their opinions."

[47:17]: "As for those who are guided, He augments their guidance, and grants them their righteousness." — the *augmentation* of guidance for those who *are already on it*.

**The disease in the hearts.**

[47:20]: "Those who believed said, 'When will a new sura be revealed?' But when a straightforward sura was revealed, and fighting was mentioned therein, you would see those who harbored doubts in their hearts looking at you, as if death had already come to them." — the *fighting-mention* in a clear sūra exposes the *doubt-carriers*.

[47:22]: "Is it also your intention that as soon as you leave you will commit evil and break your family ties?" — the *family-tie-breaking* (*qaṭ` ar-raḥim*) is a *specific* indictment.

**The deafness.**

[47:23]: God curses, deafens, blinds those who turn away.

[47:24]: "Why do they not study the Quran carefully? Do they have locks on their minds?" — *afa-lā yatadabbarūna l-qur'āna am `alā qulūbin aqfāluhā*. *Do they not contemplate the Quran, or are there locks on their hearts?* The verse is *the* foundational Quranic call to *tadabbur* — *contemplative study*. Khalifa cites this verse as a standing instruction to *engage* the text, not merely recite.

[47:25]: "Surely, those who slide back, after the guidance has been manifested to them, the devil has enticed them and led them on."

**The non-revelation of names.**

[47:30]: "If we willed, we could expose them for you, so that you can recognize them just by looking at them. However, you can recognize them by the way they talk." — *bi-laḥni l-qawl*. *By the tone of their speech*. The Quran's *politeness* — God does *not* name the hypocrites; their *speech pattern* is the *behavioral tell* the believer can read.

**The testing.**

[47:31]: "We will certainly put you to the test, in order to distinguish those among you who strive, and those who are steadfast. We will expose your true qualities."

[47:33]: "O you who believe, you shall obey GOD, and obey the messenger. Otherwise, all your works will be in vain."

**The reciprocity.**

[47:35]: "Therefore, you shall not waver and surrender in pursuit of peace, for you are guaranteed victory, and GOD is with you. He will never waste your efforts." — *wa-llāhu ma`akum*. *God is with you*. *He will never reduce your works*.

**The closing.**

[47:36]: "This worldly life is no more than play and vanity. But if you believe and lead a righteous life, He will reward you, without asking you for any money."

[47:38]: "You are invited to spend in the cause of GOD, but some of you turn stingy. The stingy with respect to himself, and GOD is the Rich, while you are the poor. If you turn away, He will substitute other people in your place, and they will not be like you." — *the substitution principle*: if this community fails, *another community* will be substituted.

**Central message in one sentence.**

The believer's works are *not wasted*; the disbeliever's works are *misdirected* ([47:1]–[47:2]); *if you help God, He helps you and steadies your feet* ([47:7]); the *four rivers of paradise* await the righteous ([47:15]); the *hypocrites are exposed by their speech-pattern*, not by name ([47:30]); *contemplative study of the Quran* is the standing instruction — *do they not contemplate*? *or are there locks on their hearts*? ([47:24]); and *if this community fails, another will be substituted* ([47:38]).

47:1.text: Those who disbelieve and repel from the path of GOD, He nullifies their works.
47:1.arabic: الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله أضل أعملهم

47:2.text: Those who believe and work righteousness, and believe in what was sent down to Muhammad - which is the truth from their Lord - He remits their sins, and blesses them with contentment.
47:2.arabic: والذين ءامنوا وعملوا الصـلحت وءامنوا بما نزل على محمد وهو الحق من ربهم كفر عنهم سيـاتهم وأصلح بالهم

47:3.text: This is because those who disbelieve are following falsehood, while those who believe are following the truth from their Lord. GOD thus cites for the people their examples.
47:3.arabic: ذلك بأن الذين كفروا اتبعوا البطل وأن الذين ءامنوا اتبعوا الحق من ربهم كذلك يضرب الله للناس أمثلهم

47:4.text: If you encounter (in war) those who disbelieve, you may strike the necks. If you take them as captives you may set them free or ransom them, until the war ends. Had GOD willed, He could have granted you victory, without war. But He thus tests you by one another. As for those who get killed in the cause of GOD, He will never put their sacrifice to waste.
47:4.arabic: فإذا لقيتم الذين كفروا فضرب الرقاب حتى إذا أثخنتموهم فشدوا الوثاق فإما منا بعد وإما فداء حتى تضع الحرب أوزارها ذلك ولو يشاء الله لانتصر منهم ولكن ليبلوا بعضكم ببعض والذين قتلوا في سبيل الله فلن يضل أعملهم

47:7.text: O you who believe, if you support GOD, He will support you, and strengthen your foothold.
47:7.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا إن تنصروا الله ينصركم ويثبت أقدامكم

47:10.text: Did they not roam the earth and see the consequences for those before them? GOD destroyed their works; all disbelievers will suffer the same fate.
47:10.arabic: ۞أفلم يسيروا في الأرض فينظروا كيف كان عقبة الذين من قبلهم دمر الله عليهم وللكفرين أمثلها

47:11.text: This is because GOD is the Lord of those who believe, while the disbelievers have no lord.
47:11.arabic: ذلك بأن الله مولى الذين ءامنوا وأن الكفرين لا مولى لهم

47:12.text: GOD admits those who believe and lead a righteous life into gardens with flowing streams. As for those who disbelieve, they live and eat like the animals eat, then end up in the hellfire.
47:12.arabic: إن الله يدخل الذين ءامنوا وعملوا الصـلحت جنـت تجري من تحتها الأنهر والذين كفروا يتمتعون ويأكلون كما تأكل الأنعم والنار مثوى لهم

47:15.text: The allegory of Paradise that is promised for the righteous is this: it has rivers of unpolluted water, and rivers of fresh milk, and rivers of wine - delicious for the drinkers - and rivers of strained honey. They have all kinds of fruits therein, and forgiveness from their Lord. (Are they better) or those who abide forever in the hellfire, and drink hellish water that tears up their intestines?
47:15.arabic: مثل الجنة التي وعد المتقون فيها أنهر من ماء غير ءاسن وأنهر من لبن لم يتغير طعمه وأنهر من خمر لذة للشـربين وأنهر من عسل مصفى ولهم فيها من كل الثمرت ومغفرة من ربهم كمن هو خلد في النار وسقوا ماء حميما فقطع أمعاءهم

47:17.text: As for those who are guided, He augments their guidance, and grants them their righteousness.
47:17.arabic: والذين اهتدوا زادهم هدى وءاتىهم تقوىهم

47:19.text: You shall know that: "There is no other god beside GOD," and ask forgiveness of your sins and the sins of all believing men and women. GOD is fully aware of your decisions and your ultimate destiny.
47:19.arabic: فاعلم أنه لا إله إلا الله واستغفر لذنبك وللمؤمنين والمؤمنت والله يعلم متقلبكم ومثوىكم

47:22.text: Is it also your intention that as soon as you leave you will commit evil and mistreat your relatives?
47:22.arabic: فهل عسيتم إن توليتم أن تفسدوا في الأرض وتقطعوا أرحامكم

47:24.text: Why do they not study the Quran carefully? Do they have locks on their minds?
47:24.arabic: أفلا يتدبرون القرءان أم على قلوب أقفالها

47:25.text: Surely, those who slide back, after the guidance has been manifested to them, the devil has enticed them and led them on.
47:25.arabic: إن الذين ارتدوا على أدبرهم من بعد ما تبين لهم الهدى الشيطن سول لهم وأملى لهم

47:30.text: If we will, we can expose them for you, so that you can recognize them just by looking at them. However, you can recognize them by the way they talk. GOD is fully aware of all your works.
47:30.arabic: ولو نشاء لأرينكهم فلعرفتهم بسيمهم ولتعرفنهم في لحن القول والله يعلم أعملكم

47:31.text: We will certainly put you to the test, in order to distinguish those among you who strive, and steadfastly persevere. We must expose your true qualities.
47:31.arabic: ولنبلونكم حتى نعلم المجهدين منكم والصـبرين ونبلوا أخباركم

47:33.text: O you who believe, you shall obey GOD, and obey the messenger. Otherwise, all your works will be in vain.
47:33.arabic: ۞يـأيها الذين ءامنوا أطيعوا الله وأطيعوا الرسول ولا تبطلوا أعملكم

47:34.text: Those who disbelieve and repel from the path of GOD, then die as disbelievers, GOD will never forgive them.
47:34.arabic: إن الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله ثم ماتوا وهم كفار فلن يغفر الله لهم

47:35.text: Therefore, you shall not waver and surrender in pursuit of peace, for you are guaranteed victory, and GOD is with you. He will never waste your efforts.
47:35.arabic: فلا تهنوا وتدعوا إلى السلم وأنتم الأعلون والله معكم ولن يتركم أعملكم

47:36.text: This worldly life is no more than play and vanity. But if you believe and lead a righteous life, He will reward you, without asking you for any money.
47:36.arabic: إنما الحيوة الدنيا لعب ولهو وإن تؤمنوا وتتقوا يؤتكم أجوركم ولا يسـلكم أمولكم

47:38.text: You are invited to spend in the cause of GOD, but some of you turn stingy. The stingy are stingy towards their own souls. GOD is Rich, while you are poor. If you turn away, He will substitute other people in your place, and they will not be like you.
47:38.arabic: هـأنتم هـؤلاء تدعون لتنفقوا في سبيل الله فمنكم من يبخل ومن يبخل فإنما يبخل عن نفسه والله الغني وأنتم الفقراء وإن تتولوا يستبدل قوما غيركم ثم لا يكونوا أمثلكم

question: What is the central message of Surah 34 (Sheba)?
answer:
Surah 34 (Saba' — "Sheba") is fifty-four verses, Meccan. The surah takes its name from [34:15], which describes the *people of Saba'* — the historical kingdom of southern Arabia, the same Sheba whose queen meets Solomon in surah 27. The surah's central message: *God's praise belongs to Him in both lives*, *David's and Solomon's blessings illustrate gratitude as the standing test*, *the Sabaean collapse illustrates the ingratitude failure*, and *the universality of the prophetic mission*.

**The opening — the praise across two lives.**

[34:1]: "Praise be to GOD — to whom belongs everything in the heavens and the earth; all praise is also due to Him in the Hereafter. He is the Most Wise, the Cognizant." — *lahu l-ḥamdu fī l-ākhirah*. *His is the praise in the Hereafter*. The verse is one of the Quran's rare claims that *God's praise extends to both worlds explicitly*.

**The book-claim — knowledge to the atomic level.**

[34:2]: "He knows everything that goes into the earth, and everything that comes out of it, and everything that comes down from the sky, and everything that climbs into it. He is the Most Merciful, Forgiver." — *yet another* claim of *omniscience* including the *atomic-scale* events.

[34:3]: "Those who disbelieve have said, 'The Hour will never come to pass!' Say, 'Absolutely — by my Lord — it will come to you. He is the Knower of the future. Not even the equivalent of an atom's weight is hidden from your Lord, be it in the heavens or the earth. Nothing smaller than that, nor larger, that is not recorded in a profound record.'" — *mithqāla dharratin*. *The weight of an atom*. The verse is one of the Quran's most-cited statements of *fine-grained divine knowledge*: *not even the weight of an atom is hidden* — *whether in the heavens or in the earth*. Combined with the preceding verse, the claim is *atom-scale knowledge of all goings-in and comings-out*.

**David's gifts.**

[34:10]: "We endowed David with blessings from us: 'O mountains, submit with him, and you too, O birds.' We softened the iron for him." — *alanna lahu l-ḥadīd*. *We softened iron for him*. The image: *the smith's gift*.

[34:11]: "Making complete coats of mail, and measure the rings perfectly. And do righteous works, all of you. I am Seer of everything you do." — the *precision instruction*: *measure the rings perfectly*. *Work righteousness as well*.

**Solomon's gifts.**

[34:12]: "To Solomon we committed the wind at his disposal, traveling one month coming and one month going. And we caused a spring of oil to gush out for him. Also, the jinns worked for him." — the *wind* with *one-month travel range each way*; the *spring of molten copper*; the *jinns* at labor.

[34:13]: "They made for him anything he wanted — niches, statues, deep pools, and heavy cooking pots. 'O family of David, work (righteousness) to show your appreciation. Only a few of My servants are appreciative.'" — *the household instruction*: *work in gratitude*.

[34:14]: "When the appointed time for his death came, nothing indicated to them that he had died until an earthworm ate (his staff). When he fell down, the jinns realized that if they really knew the unseen, they would have stopped working for Solomon." — the *theological correction*: the jinns *did not know the unseen*.

**The Sabaean collapse.**

[34:15]: "The people of Sheba used to have a wonder in their homeland: two gardens, on the right and the left. Eat from your Lord's provisions, and be appreciative of Him — good land, and a forgiving Lord." — *baldatun ṭayyibatun wa-rabbun ghafūr*. The Sabaean condition before the collapse: *good land + forgiving Lord*.

[34:16]: "They turned away. Consequently, we poured upon them a disastrous flood, and we substituted their two gardens into two gardens of bad-tasting fruits, thistles, and a skimpy harvest." — *the dam burst* (*sayl al-`arim*). *Two gardens of good fruit* become *two gardens of bitterness*.

[34:17]: "We thus requited them for their disbelief. Do we requite except the disbelievers?" — *the requital is for disbelief*, framed as ingratitude.

[34:18]–[34:19]: "We placed between them and the communities that we blessed other oases, and we secured the journey between them: 'Travel therein day and night in complete security.' But they (became spoiled and) challenged: 'Our Lord, we do not care if You increase the distance of our journeys (without any stations).' They thus wronged their own souls." — the Sabaeans' *complaint about easy travel* — they actually *asked for* longer journeys with less security. The Quran reads this as *ingratitude for convenience*. The diagnosis: *they wronged their own souls*.

**The universal messenger.**

[34:28]: "We have sent you to all the people, as a bearer of good news, as well as a warner, but most people do not know." — *wa-mā arsalnāka illā kāffatan li-n-nās*. *We have sent you for the entirety of humanity*. The verse is one of the Quran's clearest *universalist* claims about the Prophet's mission: *not just for Arabs, not just for Quraysh* — *for all people*.

**The intercession-cap.**

[34:23]: "Intercession with Him will be in vain, unless it coincides with His will." — the *intercession-permission* clause: only by God's permission.

**The leader-and-followers dialogue.**

[34:31]–[34:33]: The *future regret-dialogue* between the deceived followers and their misleading leaders, both in hell. The followers say *you misled us*; the leaders say *you were the agents of your own straying*. Both incurred punishment.

**The wealth-and-children critique.**

[34:36]–[34:37]: "Their money and children are not the means of getting closer to us. Only those who believe and lead a righteous life will receive the reward for their works, multiplied. They will reside in peaceful mansions." — *wealth and children do not approach you to God*.

**The closing — the substitution principle and the unanswerable challenge.**

[34:39]: "Say, 'My Lord is the One who controls all provisions; He grants the provisions to whomever He chooses from among His servants, and reduces for whomever. Anything you spend (in the cause of GOD), He will reward you for it; He is the best Provider.'"

[34:46]: "Say, 'I ask you to do one thing: Devote yourselves to GOD, in pairs or as individuals, then reflect.'" — *qul innamā a`iẓukum bi-wāḥidah*. *I exhort you to one thing*: *stand up for God, in twos or singly, then reflect*. One of the Quran's most-cited *single-instruction* verses.

[34:49]: "Say, 'The truth has come; while falsehood can neither initiate anything, nor repeat it.'" — *qad jā'a l-ḥaqq, wa-mā yubdi'u l-bāṭilu wa-mā yu`īd*. *Truth has come; falsehood neither originates nor repeats*. The asymmetry of *truth* and *bāṭil*.

[34:50]: "Say, 'If I go astray, I go astray because of my own shortcomings. And if I am guided, it is because of my Lord's inspiration. He is Hearer, Near.'" — Joseph's-style self-deprecation transferred to the Prophet's pulpit.

**Central message in one sentence.**

God's praise is *in both lives* and His knowledge is *to the atomic level* ([34:1]–[34:3]); David and Solomon model *gratitude under extraordinary endowment* ([34:10]–[34:14]); the *people of Saba'* model *ingratitude leading to collapse* — they had *good land + forgiving Lord* and *threw it away* ([34:15]–[34:19]); the Prophet is sent *for all of humanity* ([34:28]); *wealth and children do not save* ([34:37]); *stand up for God in twos or singly, then reflect* ([34:46]); *truth has come and falsehood neither originates nor repeats* ([34:49]).

34:1.text: Praise be to GOD - to whom belongs everything in the heavens and the earth; all praise is also due to Him in the Hereafter. He is the Most Wise, the Cognizant.
34:1.arabic: الحمد لله الذي له ما في السموت وما في الأرض وله الحمد في الأخرة وهو الحكيم الخبير

34:2.text: He knows everything that goes into the earth, and everything that comes out of it, and everything that comes down from the sky, and everything that climbs into it. He is the Most Merciful, the Forgiving.
34:2.arabic: يعلم ما يلج في الأرض وما يخرج منها وما ينزل من السماء وما يعرج فيها وهو الرحيم الغفور

34:3.text: Those who disbelieve have said, "The Hour will never come to pass!" Say, "Absolutely - by my Lord - it will most certainly come to you. He is the Knower of the future. Not even the equivalent of an atom's weight is hidden from Him, be it in the heavens or the earth. Not even smaller than that, or larger (is hidden). All are in a profound record."
34:3.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا لا تأتينا الساعة قل بلى وربي لتأتينكم علم الغيب لا يعزب عنه مثقال ذرة في السموت ولا في الأرض ولا أصغر من ذلك ولا أكبر إلا في كتب مبين

34:6.text: It is evident to those who are blessed with knowledge that this revelation from your Lord to you is the truth, and that it guides to the path of the Almighty, the Most Praiseworthy.
34:6.arabic: ويرى الذين أوتوا العلم الذي أنزل إليك من ربك هو الحق ويهدي إلى صرط العزيز الحميد

34:10.text: We endowed David with blessings from us: "O mountains, submit with him, and you too, O birds." We softened the iron for him.
34:10.arabic: ۞ولقد ءاتينا داود منا فضلا يجبال أوبي معه والطير وألنا له الحديد

34:11.text: "You may make shields that fit perfectly, and work righteousness. Whatever you do, I am Seer thereof."
34:11.arabic: أن اعمل سبغت وقدر في السرد واعملوا صلحا إني بما تعملون بصير

34:12.text: To Solomon we committed the wind at his disposal, traveling one month coming and one month going. And we caused a spring of oil to gush out for him. Also, the jinns worked for him, by his Lord's leave. Any one of them who disregarded our commands, we subjected him to a severe retribution.
34:12.arabic: ولسليمن الريح غدوها شهر ورواحها شهر وأسلنا له عين القطر ومن الجن من يعمل بين يديه بإذن ربه ومن يزغ منهم عن أمرنا نذقه من عذاب السعير

34:13.text: They made for him anything he wanted - niches, statues, deep pools, and heavy cooking pots. O family of David, work (righteousness) to show your appreciation. Only a few of My servants are appreciative. Jinns' Knowledge is Limited
34:13.arabic: يعملون له ما يشاء من محريب وتمثيل وجفان كالجواب وقدور راسيت اعملوا ءال داود شكرا وقليل من عبادي الشكور

34:14.text: When the appointed time for his death came, they had no clue that he had died. Not until one of the animals tried to eat his staff, and he fell down, did the jinns realized that he was dead. They thus realized that if they really knew the unseen, they would have stopped working so hard as soon as he died.
34:14.arabic: فلما قضينا عليه الموت ما دلهم على موته إلا دابة الأرض تأكل منسأته فلما خر تبينت الجن أن لو كانوا يعلمون الغيب ما لبثوا في العذاب المهين

34:15.text: Sheba's homeland used to be a marvel, with two gardens on the right and the left. Eat from your Lord's provisions, and be appreciative of Him - good land, and a forgiving Lord.
34:15.arabic: لقد كان لسبإ في مسكنهم ءاية جنتان عن يمين وشمال كلوا من رزق ربكم واشكروا له بلدة طيبة ورب غفور

34:16.text: They turned away and, consequently, we poured upon them a disastrous flood, and we substituted their two gardens into two gardens of badtasting fruits, thorny plants, and a skimpy harvest.
34:16.arabic: فأعرضوا فأرسلنا عليهم سيل العرم وبدلنهم بجنتيهم جنتين ذواتي أكل خمط وأثل وشيء من سدر قليل

34:17.text: We thus requited them for their disbelief. Do we not requite only the disbelievers?
34:17.arabic: ذلك جزينهم بما كفروا وهل نجزي إلا الكفور

34:18.text: We placed between them and the communites that we blessed other oases, and we secured the journey between them: "Travel therein days and nights in complete security."
34:18.arabic: وجعلنا بينهم وبين القرى التي بركنا فيها قرى ظهرة وقدرنا فيها السير سيروا فيها ليالي وأياما ءامنين

34:19.text: But they (turned unappreciative and) challenged: "Our Lord, we do not care if You increase the distance of our journeys (without any stations)." They thus wronged their own souls. Consequently, we made them history, and scattered them into small communities throughout the land. This should provide lessons for those who are steadfast, appreciative.
34:19.arabic: فقالوا ربنا بعد بين أسفارنا وظلموا أنفسهم فجعلنهم أحاديث ومزقنهم كل ممزق إن في ذلك لأيت لكل صبار شكور

34:23.text: Intercession with Him will be in vain, unless it coincides with His will. When their minds are finally settled down, and they ask, "What did your Lord say," they will say, "The truth." He is the Most High, the Most Great.
34:23.arabic: ولا تنفع الشفعة عنده إلا لمن أذن له حتى إذا فزع عن قلوبهم قالوا ماذا قال ربكم قالوا الحق وهو العلي الكبير

34:28.text: We have sent you (O Rashad) to all the people, a bearer of good news, as well as a warner, but most people do not know.
34:28.arabic: وما أرسلنك إلا كافة للناس بشيرا ونذيرا ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون

34:31.text: Those who disbelieve have said, "We will not believe in this Quran, nor in the previous scriptures." If you could only envision these transgressors when they stand before their Lord! They will argue with one another back and forth. The followers will tell their leaders, "If it were not for you, we would have been believers."
34:31.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا لن نؤمن بهذا القرءان ولا بالذي بين يديه ولو ترى إذ الظـلمون موقوفون عند ربهم يرجع بعضهم إلى بعض القول يقول الذين استضعفوا للذين استكبروا لولا أنتم لكنا مؤمنين

34:36.text: Say, "My Lord is the One who controls all provisions; He grants the provisions to whomever He wills, or reduces them, but most people do not know."
34:36.arabic: قل إن ربي يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء ويقدر ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون

34:39.text: Say, "My Lord is the One who controls all provisions; He increases the provisions for whomever He chooses from among His servants, or reduces them. Anything you spend (in the cause of God), He will reward you for it; He is the Best Provider."
34:39.arabic: قل إن ربي يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء من عباده ويقدر له وما أنفقتم من شيء فهو يخلفه وهو خير الرـزقين

34:46.text: Say, "I ask you to do one thing: Devote yourselves to GOD, in pairs or as individuals, then reflect. Your friend (Rashad) is not crazy. He is a manifest warner to you, just before the advent of a terrible retribution."
34:46.arabic: ۞قل إنما أعظكم بوحدة أن تقوموا لله مثنى وفردى ثم تتفكروا ما بصاحبكم من جنة إن هو إلا نذير لكم بين يدي عذاب شديد

34:49.text: Say, "The truth has come; while falsehood can neither initiate anything, nor repeat it."
34:49.arabic: قل جاء الحق وما يبدئ البطل وما يعيد

34:50.text: Say, "If I go astray, I go astray because of my own shortcomings. And if I am guided, it is because of my Lord's inspiration. He is Hearer, Near."
34:50.arabic: قل إن ضللت فإنما أضل على نفسي وإن اهتديت فبما يوحي إلي ربي إنه سميع قريب

question: What is the central message of Surah 49 (The Walls)?
answer:
Surah 49 (Al-Ḥujurāt — "The Walls" / "The Chambers") is eighteen verses, Medinan. The surah is the Quran's most concentrated treatise on *social etiquette and intra-community ethics* — covering deference to the Prophet, verification of news, conflict mediation, prohibition of mockery, slander, suspicion, and gossip, and the categorical statement of *racial-tribal equality before God*.
