question: What does the Quran say about God's knowledge being all-encompassing?
answer:
The Quran's claim about God's knowledge is among its strongest and most consistent theological themes — running across early Meccan and late Medinan surahs alike. The claim has three load-bearing components: (1) knowledge of *all things* — including the smallest and most hidden, (2) knowledge of the *future*, particularly *what is unrevealed to anyone else*, and (3) knowledge of *the internal* — the heart's contents, the inner thoughts.

**1. The atom-scale claim.**

[34:3]: "Not even the equivalent of an atom's weight is hidden from your Lord, be it in the heavens or the earth. Nothing smaller than that, nor larger, that is not recorded in a profound record." — *mithqāla dharratin*. *The weight of an atom*. The verse asserts knowledge at *the smallest detectable scale*. The repeat at [10:61] expands: "Whether your concerns are about the recitation of the Quran, or about any deeds you perform, we witness you when you make it your business. Not even a tiny atom in the heavens, or on earth, is hidden from your Lord. Not even smaller than that, nor larger that is not recorded in a profound record."

**2. The leaf-and-grain claim.**

[6:59]: "With Him are the keys to all secrets; none knows them except He. He knows everything on land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls without His knowledge. Nor is there a grain in the depths of the soil. Nor anything wet or dry, that is not recorded in a profound record." — *mafātīḥu l-ghayb*. *The keys of the unseen*. The verse extends *atom-scale* to *leaf-scale, grain-scale, wet-or-dry-scale*. The verse is one of the Quran's clearest *omniscience* claims and a *foundational text* for Islamic theology of *divine knowledge*.

**3. The womb-clause and the five.**

[31:34]: "With GOD is the knowledge about the Hour (end of the world). He is the One who sends down the rain, and He knows the contents of the womb. No soul knows what will happen to it tomorrow, and no one knows in which land he or she will die. GOD is Omniscient, Cognizant." — the *five exclusives*: (a) the Hour, (b) the rain's timing, (c) the womb's contents, (d) tomorrow's acquisitions, (e) the place of death. The verse lists *five categories* of knowledge that *God alone* has — none accessible to any prophet, jinn, or angel.

[13:8]: "GOD knows what every female bears, and what every womb releases, or gains. Everything He does is perfectly measured." — the *womb-knowledge* is reasserted.

**4. The thousand-witness claim.**

[58:7]: "Do you not realize that GOD knows everything in the heavens and everything on earth? No three people can conspire secretly without Him being their fourth, nor five without Him being the sixth, nor less than that, nor more, without Him being there with them wherever they may be." — the *secret-conspiracy presence*: *He is always one more than the gathering*. The verse is *numerical* in its construction — *no three without a fourth, no five without a sixth*.

**5. The closer-than-jugular claim.**

[50:16]: "We created the human, and we know what he whispers to himself. We are closer to him than his jugular vein." — *aqrabu ilayhi min ḥabli l-warīd*. *Closer to him than his jugular vein*. The verse asserts *anatomical proximity* — God's knowledge of the *self-whisper* is rendered through the *physiological* image of *closeness to the artery*.

**6. The chests' contents.**

[3:5]: "Nothing is hidden from GOD, on earth, or in the heaven." — generic.

[35:38]: "GOD is the Knower of the future of the heavens and the earth. He is the Knower of the innermost thoughts." — *`ālim al-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādah*. *Knower of the unseen and the witnessed*. The phrase recurs ten times in the Quran, almost a divine epithet.

[67:14]: "Should He not know what He created? He is the Sublime, Most Cognizant." — *a-lā ya`lamu man khalaq*. The *epistemological argument*: *the One who created you must know you*.

**7. The 'Throne-Verse' summary.**

[2:255] (Āyat al-Kursī — the Throne Verse): "GOD: there is no other god besides Him, the Living, the Eternal. Never a moment of unawareness or slumber overtakes Him. To Him belongs everything in the heavens and everything on earth. Who could intercede with Him, except in accordance with His will? He knows their past, and their future. No one acquires any knowledge, except as He wills. His dominion encompasses the heavens and the earth, and ruling them never burdens Him. He is the Most High, the Great." — the *no-slumber-or-sleep* clause + *He knows what is between their hands and what is behind them* + *they encompass nothing of His knowledge except what He wills*. The Throne Verse is the *most-cited single statement* in the Quran on the *omniscience-omnipotence* unity.

**8. The future-disclosure exception.**

[72:26]–[72:27]: "He is the Knower of the future; He does not reveal the future to anyone. Only to a messenger that He chooses, does He reveal from the past and the future, specific news. He carefully guards the (revealed news) in front of him and behind him." — the *general* rule: *no creature has access to the future*. The *exception*: *chosen messengers receive specific news*, with *escort angels*. This is a critical Quranic claim for distinguishing *prophetic foresight* from any *general magical-future-divination*.

[27:65]: "Say, 'No one in the heavens or the earth knows the future except GOD. They do not even perceive how or when they will be resurrected.'" — *qul lā ya`lamu man fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi l-ghayba illā llāh*. *No one in the heavens or earth knows the future except God*.

**9. The soul-knowledge limit.**

[17:85]: "They ask you about the revelation. Say, 'The revelation comes from my Lord. The knowledge given to you is minute.'" — *wa-mā ūtītum mina l-`ilmi illā qalīlā*. *You have not been given of knowledge but a little*. The verse asserts *the contrast*: God's knowledge is *complete*, human knowledge *little*.

**10. The hidden agenda exposed on the Day.**

[20:7]: "Whether you declare your convictions (or not), He knows the secret, and what is even more hidden." — *as-sirra wa-akhfā*. *The secret and what is even more hidden*. *Akhfā* (lit. "more hidden") suggests a *second layer* below conscious secrets — what is *hidden even from yourself*.

[57:3]: "He is the Alpha and the Omega. He is the Outermost and the Innermost. He is fully aware of all things." — *al-Awwalu wa-l-Ākhiru wa-ẓ-Ẓāhiru wa-l-Bāṭin*. *The First, the Last, the Outermost, the Innermost*. The four-name sequence is one of the Quran's strongest *omnipresence-omniscience* combinations.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's claim is *exhaustive*: God's knowledge encompasses (a) the *atom-scale* ([34:3], [10:61]); (b) the *leaf-and-grain scale* ([6:59]); (c) the *womb's contents* ([31:34], [13:8]); (d) *every secret conspiracy*, He being always one more than the gathering ([58:7]); (e) the *self-whisper*, with *anatomical proximity* metaphor ([50:16]); (f) the *innermost thoughts of chests* ([35:38]); (g) the *past and future*, with no *future-access* for any creature except *chosen messengers under angelic escort* ([72:26]–[72:27]); (h) *what is more hidden than your own secrets* ([20:7]); and (i) is summarized in the *Throne Verse*'s *no-slumber-or-sleep + knowing-between-their-hands-and-behind-them* ([2:255]). Human knowledge is, by contrast, *little* ([17:85]).

2:255.text: GOD: there is no other god besides Him, the Living, the Eternal. Never a moment of unawareness or slumber overtakes Him. To Him belongs everything in the heavens and everything on earth. Who could intercede with Him, except in accordance with His will? He knows their past, and their future. No one attains any knowledge, except as He wills. His dominion encompasses the heavens and the earth, and ruling them never burdens Him. He is the Most High, the Great.
2:255.arabic: الله لا إله إلا هو الحي القيوم لا تأخذه سنة ولا نوم له ما في السموت وما في الأرض من ذا الذي يشفع عنده إلا بإذنه يعلم ما بين أيديهم وما خلفهم ولا يحيطون بشيء من علمه إلا بما شاء وسع كرسيه السموت والأرض ولا يـوده حفظهما وهو العلي العظيم

3:5.text: Nothing is hidden from GOD, on earth, or in the heaven.
3:5.arabic: إن الله لا يخفى عليه شيء في الأرض ولا في السماء

6:59.text: With Him are the keys to all secrets; none knows them except He. He knows everything on land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls without His knowledge. Nor is there a grain in the depths of the soil. Nor is there anything wet or dry, that is not recorded in a profound record. Death and Resurrection: Every Day
6:59.arabic: ۞وعنده مفاتح الغيب لا يعلمها إلا هو ويعلم ما في البر والبحر وما تسقط من ورقة إلا يعلمها ولا حبة في ظلمت الأرض ولا رطب ولا يابس إلا في كتب مبين

6:103.text: No visions can encompass Him, but He encompasses all visions. He is the Compassionate, the Cognizant.
6:103.arabic: لا تدركه الأبصر وهو يدرك الأبصر وهو اللطيف الخبير

10:61.text: You do not get into any situation, nor do you recite any Quran, nor do you do anything, without us being witnesses thereof as you do it. Not even an atom's weight is out of your Lord's control, be it in the heavens or the earth. Nor is there anything smaller than an atom, or larger, that is not recorded in a profound record. Happiness: Now and Forever
10:61.arabic: وما تكون في شأن وما تتلوا منه من قرءان ولا تعملون من عمل إلا كنا عليكم شهودا إذ تفيضون فيه وما يعزب عن ربك من مثقال ذرة في الأرض ولا في السماء ولا أصغر من ذلك ولا أكبر إلا في كتب مبين

13:8.text: GOD knows what every female bears, and what every womb releases, or gains. Everything He does is perfectly measured.
13:8.arabic: الله يعلم ما تحمل كل أنثى وما تغيض الأرحام وما تزداد وكل شيء عنده بمقدار

13:9.text: The Knower of all secrets and declarations; the Supreme, the Most High.

17:85.text: They ask you about the revelation. Say, "The revelation comes from my Lord. The knowledge given to you is minute."
17:85.arabic: ويسـلونك عن الروح قل الروح من أمر ربي وما أوتيتم من العلم إلا قليلا

20:7.text: Whether you declare your convictions (or not) He knows the secret, and what is even more hidden.
20:7.arabic: وإن تجهر بالقول فإنه يعلم السر وأخفى

27:65.text: Say, "No one in the heavens and the earth knows the future except GOD. They do not even perceive how or when they will be resurrected." Believing in the Hereafter: Great Obstacle for Most People
27:65.arabic: قل لا يعلم من في السموت والأرض الغيب إلا الله وما يشعرون أيان يبعثون

31:34.text: With GOD is the knowledge about the Hour (end of the world). He is the One who sends down the rain, and He knows the contents of the womb. No soul knows what will happen to it tomorrow, and no one knows in which land he or she will die. GOD is Omniscient, Cognizant.
31:34.arabic: إن الله عنده علم الساعة وينزل الغيث ويعلم ما في الأرحام وما تدري نفس ماذا تكسب غدا وما تدري نفس بأي أرض تموت إن الله عليم خبير

34:2.text: He knows everything that goes into the earth, and everything that comes out of it, and everything that comes down from the sky, and everything that climbs into it. He is the Most Merciful, the Forgiving.
34:2.arabic: يعلم ما يلج في الأرض وما يخرج منها وما ينزل من السماء وما يعرج فيها وهو الرحيم الغفور

34:3.text: Those who disbelieve have said, "The Hour will never come to pass!" Say, "Absolutely - by my Lord - it will most certainly come to you. He is the Knower of the future. Not even the equivalent of an atom's weight is hidden from Him, be it in the heavens or the earth. Not even smaller than that, or larger (is hidden). All are in a profound record."
34:3.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا لا تأتينا الساعة قل بلى وربي لتأتينكم علم الغيب لا يعزب عنه مثقال ذرة في السموت ولا في الأرض ولا أصغر من ذلك ولا أكبر إلا في كتب مبين

35:11.text: GOD created you from dust, then from a tiny drop, then He causes you to reproduce through your spouses. No female becomes pregnant, nor gives birth, without His knowledge. No one survives for a long life, and no one's life is snapped short, except in accordance with a pre-existing record. This is easy for GOD.
35:11.arabic: والله خلقكم من تراب ثم من نطفة ثم جعلكم أزوجا وما تحمل من أنثى ولا تضع إلا بعلمه وما يعمر من معمر ولا ينقص من عمره إلا في كتب إن ذلك على الله يسير

49:16.text: Say, "Are you informing GOD about your religion? GOD knows everything in the heavens and the earth. GOD is Omniscient." Who Is Doing Whom A Favor?
49:16.arabic: قل أتعلمون الله بدينكم والله يعلم ما في السموت وما في الأرض والله بكل شيء عليم

50:16.text: We created the human, and we know what he whispers to himself. We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
50:16.arabic: ولقد خلقنا الإنسن ونعلم ما توسوس به نفسه ونحن أقرب إليه من حبل الوريد

57:3.text: He is the Alpha and the Omega. He is the Outermost and the Innermost. He is fully aware of all things.
57:3.arabic: هو الأول والأخر والظـهر والباطن وهو بكل شيء عليم

58:7.text: Do you not realize that GOD knows everything in the heavens and everything on earth? No three people can conspire secretly without Him being their fourth, nor five without Him being the sixth, nor less than that, nor more, without Him being there with them wherever they may be. Then, on the Day of Resurrection, He will inform them of everything they had done. GOD is fully aware of all things.
58:7.arabic: ألم تر أن الله يعلم ما في السموت وما في الأرض ما يكون من نجوى ثلثة إلا هو رابعهم ولا خمسة إلا هو سادسهم ولا أدنى من ذلك ولا أكثر إلا هو معهم أين ما كانوا ثم ينبئهم بما عملوا يوم القيمة إن الله بكل شيء عليم

65:12.text: GOD created seven universes and the same number of earths. The commands flow among them. This is to let you know that GOD is Omnipotent, and that GOD is fully aware of all things.
65:12.arabic: الله الذي خلق سبع سموت ومن الأرض مثلهن يتنزل الأمر بينهن لتعلموا أن الله على كل شيء قدير وأن الله قد أحاط بكل شيء علما

67:14.text: Should He not know what He created? He is the Sublime, Most Cognizant.
67:14.arabic: ألا يعلم من خلق وهو اللطيف الخبير

question: How does the Quran portray God's justice (`adl)?
answer:
The Quran's portrayal of God's justice is built around three interlocking claims: (1) *no soul is wronged in the slightest* — every act is repaid in *exact* measure, (2) *justice is the standing requirement on humans*, modeled on God's own *just-judgment*, and (3) *messengers and scriptures are sent that humans may uphold justice*.

**1. The *atom-of-injustice* principle.**

[4:40]: "GOD does not inflict an atom's weight of injustice. On the contrary, He multiplies the reward manyfold for the righteous work, and grants from Him a great recompense." — *inna llāha lā yaẓlimu mithqāla dharrah*. *God does not wrong by the weight of an atom*. The verse is *the* foundational Quranic claim about God's *non-injustice*: not *barely just*, but *literally not wronging by even atomic measure*.

[10:44]: "GOD never wrongs the people; it is the people who wrong their own souls." — *innā llāha lā yaẓlimu n-nāsa shay'an wa-lākinna n-nāsa anfusahum yaẓlimūn*. The *causal attribution*: *self-wrong* is the diagnosis; *God-wrong* is impossible.

**2. The exact-measure recompense.**

[6:160]: "Whoever does a good work receives the reward for ten, and the one who commits a sin is requited only for one. No one suffers the slightest injustice." — *the asymmetry*: good works *multiplied by ten*; bad works *requited as one*. The asymmetry is *itself* a *form of mercy operating within justice*.

[7:8]–[7:9]: "The scales will be set on that day, equitably. Those whose weights are heavy will be the winners. As for those whose weights are light, they will be the ones who lost their souls, because they disregarded our revelations, unjustly." — the *scales of deeds* (*al-mawāzīn*) operate *equitably* — *bi-l-qisṭ*.

[21:47]: "We will establish the scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection. No soul will suffer the least injustice. Even the equivalent of a mustard seed will be accounted for. We are the most efficient reckoners." — *mawāzīn al-qisṭ*. *The scales of equity*. The *mustard seed* (*khardal*) is named — *the smallest unit accounted for*. The verse is the strongest single statement of *fine-grained accounting*: *not even a mustard seed escapes*.

[39:69]: "Then the earth will shine with the light of its Lord. The record will be proclaimed, and the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forth. Everyone will then be judged equitably, without the least injustice." — *yuqḍā baynahum bi-l-ḥaqq*. *Judged in truth*.

[46:19]: "They have attained different ranks, for what each one did. They will be fully paid for their works, without the least injustice." — *li-yuwaffiyahum a`mālahum wa-hum lā yuẓlamūn*. *Paid in full*, *not wronged*.

**3. No vicarious atonement, no transferred merit.**

The justice principle implies that *no soul carries another's burden* — repeated at six places (6:164, 17:15, 35:18, 39:7, 53:38) — and is *correlated* with the *atom-of-injustice* claim. If God *does not wrong*, He *also does not transfer the deeds of one onto another*.

[17:71]: "The day will come when we summon every people, together with their record. As for those who are given their record of righteousness, they will read their record and will not suffer the least injustice." — *ulā'ika yaqra'ūna kitābahum wa-lā yuẓlamūna fatīlā*. *They read their record and are not wronged by the slimmest filament*. *Fatīl* — the *thread between the date-stone's two halves* — proverbially the smallest amount.

**4. Justice as the standing requirement on humans.**

[4:135]: "O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses, even against yourselves, or your parents, or your relatives. Whether the accused is rich or poor, GOD takes care of both. Therefore, do not be biased by your personal wishes. If you deviate, distort your testimony, or refuse to testify, then know that GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do." — *kūnū qawwāmīna bi-l-qisṭi shuhadā'a li-llāhi wa-law `alā anfusikum*. *Be standing for equity, witnesses for God, even against yourselves*. The verse names the *most extreme* case: *testify against yourselves, parents, relatives* if equity requires.

[5:8]: "O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses. Do not be provoked by your conflicts with some people into committing injustice. You shall be absolutely equitable, for it is more righteous. You shall observe GOD. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do." — *wa-lā yajrimannakum shana'ānu qawmin `alā allā ta`dilū*. *Do not let the hatred of any people lead you not to be just*. The verse is the *anti-collective-bias* clause: *enemy-hatred is not an excuse for injustice*.

[16:90]: "GOD advocates justice, charity, and regarding the relatives. And He forbids evil, vice, and transgression. He enlightens you, that you may take heed." — *inna llāha ya'muru bi-l-`adli wa-l-iḥsāni*. *God commands justice and excellence*. The *triple positive* (*`adl, iḥsān, ītā'i dhī al-qurbā*) + the *triple negative* (*faḥshā', munkar, baghī*). The verse is one of the most-cited Quranic *ethical capsules*.

**5. The witness-against-the-self clause.**

[3:18]: "GOD bears witness that there is no god except He, and so do the angels and those who possess knowledge. Truthfully and equitably, He is the absolute god; there is no god but He, the Almighty, Most Wise." — *qā'iman bi-l-qisṭ*. *Standing in equity*. The verse names *equity* as a divine *attribute*.

**6. The iron-and-the-scale claim.**

[57:25]: "We sent our messengers supported by clear proofs, and we sent down to them the scripture and the law, that the people may uphold justice. And we sent down the iron, wherein there is strength, and many benefits for the people. All this in order for GOD to distinguish those who would support Him and His messengers, on faith. GOD is Powerful, Almighty." — *li-yaqūma n-nāsu bi-l-qisṭ*. *That the people may uphold justice*. The verse names *justice* as the *operational goal* of the *scripture-and-balance* package. Messengers come *for justice* — and *the iron* is co-sent, providing *both strength and benefits* — a unique pairing of *scripture* and *metallurgy* in a single verse.

**7. Justice in the disputes-of-the-People-of-the-Book.**

[5:42]: "If they come to you to judge among them, you may judge among them, or you may disregard them. If you choose to disregard them, they cannot harm you in the least. But if you judge among them, you shall judge equitably. GOD loves those who are equitable." — *fa-aḥkum baynahum bi-l-qisṭi inna llāha yuḥibbu l-muqsiṭīn*. *God loves the equitable*. The verse extends the *equity* requirement to *interreligious legal contexts*: even if the cross-religious dispute could be ignored, if accepted, must be *equitably* judged.

**8. The hand-tongue-foot witness.**

[24:24]: "The day will come when their own tongues, hands, and feet will bear witness to everything they had done." — the *body parts witness*: *every limb testifies* to what the soul did with it. *No injustice* is possible *when the witnesses are the agent's own organs*.

**9. The day-by-day distribution.**

[40:17]: "On that day, every soul will be requited for whatever it had earned. There will be no injustice on that day. GOD is most efficient in reckoning." — *al-yawma tujzā kullu nafsin bi-mā kasabat lā ẓulma l-yawma*. *Today, every soul is repaid for what it earned; no injustice today*. *Today* the impossibility of injustice is asserted *categorically*.

**Synthesis.**

The Quran's portrayal of God's justice is built on: *not an atom's weight of injustice* ([4:40]), *no soul wrongs except its own self* ([10:44]), *good works rewarded ten-fold, bad works one-fold* ([6:160]), *the mustard-seed accounting* ([21:47]), *the witness-against-yourself requirement* ([4:135]), *the enemy-hatred is no excuse* ([5:8]), *the triple-command of justice-excellence-kin-regard* ([16:90]), *the scripture-and-iron sent for justice* ([57:25]), *the body-parts-as-witnesses* ([24:24]), and *the no-injustice-today verdict* ([40:17]). The cumulative portrait: *the standard human aspires to* equity, *the divine reality is* equity, and *the disparity between the two* is the *moral ground* of the Quran's prescriptive ethics.

3:18.text: GOD bears witness that there is no god except He, and so do the angels and those who possess knowledge. Truthfully and equitably, He is the absolute god; there is no god but He, the Almighty, Most Wise.
3:18.arabic: شهد الله أنه لا إله إلا هو والملـئكة وأولوا العلم قائما بالقسط لا إله إلا هو العزيز الحكيم

4:40.text: GOD does not inflict an atom's weight of injustice. On the contrary, He multiplies the reward manifold for the righteous work, and grants from Him a great recompense.
4:40.arabic: إن الله لا يظلم مثقال ذرة وإن تك حسنة يضعفها ويؤت من لدنه أجرا عظيما

4:135.text: O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses, even against yourselves, or your parents, or your relatives. Whether the accused is rich or poor, GOD takes care of both. Therefore, do not be biased by your personal wishes. If you deviate or disregard (this commandment), then GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
4:135.arabic: ۞يـأيها الذين ءامنوا كونوا قوـمين بالقسط شهداء لله ولو على أنفسكم أو الولدين والأقربين إن يكن غنيا أو فقيرا فالله أولى بهما فلا تتبعوا الهوى أن تعدلوا وإن تلوا أو تعرضوا فإن الله كان بما تعملون خبيرا

5:8.text: O you who believe, you shall be absolutely equitable, and observe GOD, when you serve as witnesses. Do not be provoked by your conflicts with some people into committing injustice. You shall be absolutely equitable, for it is more righteous. You shall observe GOD. GOD is fully Cognizant of everything you do.
5:8.arabic: يـأيها الذين ءامنوا كونوا قوـمين لله شهداء بالقسط ولا يجرمنكم شنـان قوم على ألا تعدلوا اعدلوا هو أقرب للتقوى واتقوا الله إن الله خبير بما تعملون

5:42.text: They are upholders of lies, and eaters of illicit earnings. If they come to you to judge among them, you may judge among them, or you may disregard them. If you choose to disregard them, they cannot harm you in the least. But if you judge among them, you shall judge equitably. GOD loves those who are equitable.
5:42.arabic: سمـعون للكذب أكـلون للسحت فإن جاءوك فاحكم بينهم أو أعرض عنهم وإن تعرض عنهم فلن يضروك شيـا وإن حكمت فاحكم بينهم بالقسط إن الله يحب المقسطين

6:160.text: Whoever does a righteous work receives the reward for ten, and the one who commits a sin is requited for only one. No one suffers the slightest injustice.
6:160.arabic: من جاء بالحسنة فله عشر أمثالها ومن جاء بالسيئة فلا يجزى إلا مثلها وهم لا يظلمون

7:8.text: The scales will be set on that day, equitably. Those whose weights are heavy will be the winners.
7:8.arabic: والوزن يومئذ الحق فمن ثقلت موزينه فأولـئك هم المفلحون

7:9.text: As for those whose weights are light, they will be the ones who lost their souls as a consequence of disregarding our revelations, unjustly.
7:9.arabic: ومن خفت موزينه فأولـئك الذين خسروا أنفسهم بما كانوا بـايتنا يظلمون

10:44.text: GOD never wrongs the people; it is the people who wrong their own souls.
10:44.arabic: إن الله لا يظلم الناس شيـا ولكن الناس أنفسهم يظلمون

10:47.text: To each community, a messenger. After their messenger comes, they are judged equitably, without the least injustice.
10:47.arabic: ولكل أمة رسول فإذا جاء رسولهم قضي بينهم بالقسط وهم لا يظلمون

10:54.text: If any wicked soul possessed everything on earth, it would readily offer it as ransom. They will be ridden with remorse when they see the retribution. They will be judged equitably, without the least injustice.
10:54.arabic: ولو أن لكل نفس ظلمت ما في الأرض لافتدت به وأسروا الندامة لما رأوا العذاب وقضي بينهم بالقسط وهم لا يظلمون

16:90.text: GOD advocates justice, charity, and regarding the relatives. And He forbids evil, vice, and transgression. He enlightens you, that you may take heed.
16:90.arabic: ۞إن الله يأمر بالعدل والإحسن وإيتاي ذي القربى وينهى عن الفحشاء والمنكر والبغي يعظكم لعلكم تذكرون

17:71.text: The day will come when we summon every people, together with their record. As for those who are given a record of righteousness, they will read their record and will not suffer the least injustice.
17:71.arabic: يوم ندعوا كل أناس بإممهم فمن أوتي كتبه بيمينه فأولـئك يقرءون كتبهم ولا يظلمون فتيلا

21:47.text: We will establish the scales of justice on the Day of Resurrection. No soul will suffer the least injustice. Even the equivalent of a mustard seed will be accounted for. We are the most efficient reckoners.
21:47.arabic: ونضع الموزين القسط ليوم القيمة فلا تظلم نفس شيـا وإن كان مثقال حبة من خردل أتينا بها وكفى بنا حسبين

23:62.text: We never burden any soul beyond its means, and we keep a record that utters the truth. No one will suffer injustice.
23:62.arabic: ولا نكلف نفسا إلا وسعها ولدينا كتب ينطق بالحق وهم لا يظلمون

39:69.text: Then the earth will shine with the light of its Lord. The record will be proclaimed, and the prophets and the witnesses will be brought forth. Everyone will then be judged equitably, without the least injustice.
39:69.arabic: وأشرقت الأرض بنور ربها ووضع الكتب وجايء بالنبيـن والشهداء وقضي بينهم بالحق وهم لا يظلمون

40:17.text: On that day, every soul will be requited for whatever it had earned. There will be no injustice on that day. GOD is most efficient in reckoning.
40:17.arabic: اليوم تجزى كل نفس بما كسبت لا ظلم اليوم إن الله سريع الحساب

45:22.text: GOD created the heavens and the earth for a specific purpose, in order to pay each soul for whatever it earned, without the least injustice.
45:22.arabic: وخلق الله السموت والأرض بالحق ولتجزى كل نفس بما كسبت وهم لا يظلمون

46:19.text: They all attain the ranks they have deserved, in accordance with their works. He will pay them for their works, without the least injustice.
46:19.arabic: ولكل درجت مما عملوا وليوفيهم أعملهم وهم لا يظلمون

57:25.text: We sent our messengers supported by clear proofs, and we sent down to them the scripture and the law, that the people may uphold justice. And we sent down the iron, wherein there is strength, and many benefits for the people. All this in order for GOD to distinguish those who would support Him and His messengers, on faith. GOD is Powerful, Almighty.
57:25.arabic: لقد أرسلنا رسلنا بالبينت وأنزلنا معهم الكتب والميزان ليقوم الناس بالقسط وأنزلنا الحديد فيه بأس شديد ومنفع للناس وليعلم الله من ينصره ورسله بالغيب إن الله قوي عزيز
